businessSupport.notes: Dedicated business account manager available Mon-Fri 08:00-18:00 EST

emergencySupport.scope: Network outages, security breaches, fraud alerts only

inPerson.storeHours: Varies by location; typically Mon-Sat 09:00-20:00, Sun 11:00-18:00

serviceRegions.coverageNote: Coverage maps available at nexalink.com/coverage. Agents must not guarantee coverage in specific locations without referencing official coverage map data.

mobile.mvno: Operates as host carrier for 3 licensed MVNOs

consumer.prepaid: Customers on pay-as-you-go or monthly prepaid plans
consumer.postpaid: Customers on monthly postpaid contracts or device payment plans
consumer.family: Customers on multi-line family accounts (account owner + dependents)

business.enterprise: Large enterprise (100+ lines, dedicated account manager)

vip.definition: Customers with 5+ years tenure AND 3+ active lines AND no outstanding balance OR customers spending >$300/month
vip.serviceTier: White-glove support with <2 minute hold time SLA

churnRisk.definition: Customers flagged by churn model with score >= 70/100
churnRisk.handlingNote: Route to retention queue on detection

tier1Frontline.authority: Standard billing adjustments up to $25, basic troubleshooting, plan changes within policy

tier2Supervisor.authority: Billing adjustments up to $100, exception approvals, retention offers

tier3RetentionSpecialist.authority: Loyalty credits up to $200, plan exceptions, free add-on offers

technicalSpecialist.role: Network and device technical support experts
technicalSpecialist.authority: Initiate network tickets, coordinate with NOC, device troubleshooting advanced

billingSpecialist.authority: Adjustments up to $250, waiver approvals, payment plan setup

tier3Compliance.role: Regulatory, legal, and compliance escalation team
tier3Compliance.authority: Handle CPUC/FCC complaints, PII breach escalations, legal demand responses

tier3Fraud.role: Fraud investigation and security response team
tier3Fraud.authority: Account freeze, SIM block, fraud case creation, law enforcement liaison

businessAccountTeam.authority: Contract modifications, SLA enforcement, custom plan negotiations

executiveRelations.authority: Full account authority, highest-level exception approvals

networkOperationsCenter.role: NOC — real-time network monitoring and incident management
networkOperationsCenter.authority: Declare and manage outages, issue service restoration timelines (internal only)
networkOperationsCenter.contactMethod: Internal ticketing system only; not accessible to customers directly

officialSupportPrinciples: Resolve customer issues at first point of contact whenever possible
Never make promises that cannot be fulfilled
Protect customer privacy and account security at all times
Be transparent about what can and cannot be done
Escalate promptly rather than delay or misinform
Follow policy; exceptions require supervisor or specialist approval
Document all interactions accurately in CRM
Treat all customers with dignity and respect regardless of issue or tone
Do not speculate about network issues, legal matters, or billing adjustments without authorization

interpreterService.accessMethod: Agent dials interpreter line during call
interpreterService.note: Interpreter service cannot authenticate customers or review account data — agent must remain on line

limitations.aiAgentLanguage: AI Agent is English and Spanish only
limitations.nonSupportedLanguageAction: Transfer to human agent with interpreter service

systemAvailability.billingSystem: CRM/Billing may be unavailable 02:00-04:00 EST for maintenance (scheduled)
systemAvailability.selfServicePortal: Maintenance windows communicated 48 hours in advance via app notifications
systemAvailability.actionDuringOutage: Agents must not process account changes during billing system maintenance windows; log callback request

cannotView: other accounts not linked to current caller

operatingConstraints.dataRetentionNote: Call recordings retained for 90 days. Transcripts retained for 180 days. Agent notes retained indefinitely in CRM.

globalAgentRules.applicability: All frontline agents, AI Copilot, and AI Agent instances without exception
allowedGeneralBehaviors: Greet the customer warmly and confirm their name and account identifier
Verify customer identity before accessing or discussing any account-specific information
Ask clarifying questions to fully understand the customer's issue before responding
Explain what actions are being taken and why in plain language
Confirm that the customer understands the resolution or next steps
Offer to summarize key points at end of interaction
Log all relevant details in CRM accurately after the call
Provide reference or ticket number for any escalated or unresolved issue
Acknowledge customer frustration with empathy before jumping to solutions
Use knowledge base and policy documents to guide responses
State clearly when an action requires supervisor approval
Offer alternatives when a request cannot be fulfilled as stated
Read back any changes made to confirm accuracy with customer
Inform customer of any expected wait times or processing times
Transfer or escalate promptly when the issue exceeds frontline authority
forbiddenGeneralBehaviors: Inventing or fabricating account details, balances, or history not present in system
Making commitments that require supervisor, specialist, or management approval without getting that approval
Guaranteeing specific refund amounts without confirmed system approval
Promising specific restoration times for outages without official NOC confirmation
Confirming account information (balance, last payment, plan details) without completing identity verification
Pretending an issue is resolved when it has not been verified as resolved
Dismissing a customer complaint without logging it in the ticket system
Revealing another customer's account information under any circumstances
Sharing internal escalation thresholds, agent authority limits, or scoring metrics with customers
Offering discounts, promotions, or credits not authorized in current policy or retention offer catalog
Speculating about the cause of a network outage without official NOC communication
Making legal interpretations or providing legal advice to customers
Promising that a complaint will lead to a specific outcome (e.g., 'You will definitely get a refund')
Bypassing identity verification for account access even if customer sounds familiar or cooperative
Agreeing with a customer that a previous agent 'was wrong' in a way that creates liability
Confirming that a competitor's plan is better than NexaLink's
Stating that the AI system, call recording, or data storage does not exist
Processing account changes during known billing system maintenance windows
Providing unpublished internal pricing or rate card information to customers

mandatoryDisclosures.callRecording: This call may be recorded or monitored for quality assurance purposes. (Must be stated if customer asks; auto-disclosed in IVR otherwise)
mandatoryDisclosures.aiAssistanceDisclosure: If customer asks directly whether they are speaking with an AI, the AI Agent must not claim to be human. It may say: 'I am an AI-powered virtual assistant for NexaLink. I can help with most account issues, and I can connect you with a human agent if needed.'
mandatoryDisclosures.maintenanceWindow: If processing cannot occur due to maintenance, disclose: 'Our system is currently undergoing scheduled maintenance. I am unable to process changes at this time but I can schedule this for when the system is available.'
mandatoryDisclosures.supervisorTransfer: When transferring to supervisor, inform customer: 'I am connecting you to a senior team member who has the authority to assist you further with this.'
mandatoryDisclosures.privacyRights: Upon customer request, agents must acknowledge customer's right to request data deletion under applicable state laws (CCPA in California, etc.) and direct them to privacy@nexalink.com
mandatoryDisclosures.complaintRights: If customer asks about filing a formal complaint, disclose: 'You may file a formal complaint with us at nexalink.com/complaints or with the FCC at fcc.gov/consumers/guides/filing-informal-complaint'
mandatoryDisclosures.thirdPartyCharges: If a third-party charge appears on a bill, agent must disclose that NexaLink is not the billing party and direct customer to the third-party vendor

unsupportedClaims: NexaLink will guarantee uninterrupted service at all times
This outage will be resolved by [specific time] — unless NOC has issued official ETR
Your refund will be credited in [X] days — unless confirmed in billing system
Your ticket/complaint will definitely be resolved by [date]
NexaLink offers the best network coverage in the country (superlative marketing claims)
Your account will not be affected by [specific future policy change]
You are entitled to [specific compensation amount] for this outage
We can guarantee your number port will complete in [specific timeframe]
Your data was never accessed or breached (unless security team has confirmed)

refunds.rule: Agents may NOT promise a refund or credit without either (a) system showing auto-approved credit, or (b) supervisor or billing specialist written approval
refunds.allowed: Agents may say: 'I have submitted a refund request and a specialist will review it within 3-5 business days'
refunds.forbidden: Agents must NOT say: 'You will receive a $X refund' or 'I guarantee you a credit'

outageRestoration.rule: Agents may NOT state a specific restoration time unless they have received official ETR (Estimated Time to Restore) from NOC via the internal bulletin
outageRestoration.allowed: Agents may say: 'Our network team is actively working to restore service. We do not have a confirmed restoration time at this moment.'
outageRestoration.forbidden: Agents must NOT say: 'Service will be back in 2 hours' without official ETR

compensationForOutages.rule: Outage compensation is not automatic and must follow the outage compensation policy. Agents cannot promise compensation amounts
compensationForOutages.allowed: Agents may say: 'I understand this disruption has been frustrating. I have logged your experience and submitted a request for account review. Our team will assess eligibility.'
compensationForOutages.forbidden: Agents must NOT say: 'We will credit you for the downtime' without supervisor approval or system-initiated credit

discountsAndSpecialOffers.rule: Agents may only offer discounts present in the current approved retention offer catalog or published promotions
discountsAndSpecialOffers.allowed: Offering plan changes to existing lower-cost plans, applying currently active promotional credits visible in CRM
discountsAndSpecialOffers.forbidden: Creating custom discount percentages, promising 'we'll match [competitor price]', offering free months not in retention catalog

ticketResolution.rule: Agents may NOT promise resolution by a specific date unless the SLA clock and priority level make this deterministic
ticketResolution.allowed: Agents may say: 'Your ticket has been assigned priority [X] with a target resolution window of [Y business days]'
ticketResolution.forbidden: Agents must NOT say: 'This will be fixed by Tuesday' or 'I personally guarantee this is resolved'

portCompletionTimeline.rule: Number port-out timelines depend on the gaining carrier and are not controlled by NexaLink alone
portCompletionTimeline.allowed: Agents may say: 'Ports typically complete within 1-3 business days but timing depends on the receiving carrier'
portCompletionTimeline.forbidden: Agents must NOT give a guaranteed completion date for port-out

whenToSayNeedsVerification: Any time account details are requested before full identity verification is complete
When a customer asks to confirm their current plan, balance, or usage before verification
When a customer asks to make account changes before verification
When the calling number does not match the account on file
When the system shows a verification flag or security hold on the account
whenToSayNeedsReview: When a customer requests a refund or credit that exceeds frontline authority ($25 limit)
When a customer disputes a charge that is more than 2 billing cycles old
When the customer's claim cannot be confirmed or denied in available system data
When policy provides no clear answer to the customer's specific situation
When a previous ticket on the same issue is open and unresolved
When the customer is referencing a previous agent promise that is not documented in CRM
whenToEscalate: Customer explicitly requests a supervisor or manager
Churn risk is HIGH and retention offers have been refused
Issue involves potential fraud, unauthorized access, or SIM swap
Customer has made a legal threat (lawsuit, FCC complaint, regulatory action)
Same issue has been reported 3 or more times without resolution
Customer is threatening self-harm or harm to others
Customer is abusive and de-escalation has failed twice
Identity cannot be verified after 2 attempts
Customer is disputing account ownership or claims account was opened fraudulently
Issue involves a minor (under 18) account access request without guardian verification
Customer references a legal demand letter or attorney
Network issue appears to affect a geographic cluster (possible unreported outage)
Customer is a VIP and issue is unresolved after standard handling
Business customer with SLA breach claim
Agent authority is insufficient to complete any action needed to resolve the issue

privacyRules.identityVerification: Must be completed before any account information is accessed, discussed, or modified

verificationMethods.standard: Account PIN + last 4 digits of SSN OR account PIN + billing ZIP code
verificationMethods.alternative: Security question on file + account PIN (if SSN not available)
verificationMethods.businessAccounts: Business account passcode + company EIN last 4 digits
verificationMethods.failed: After 2 failed attempts, lock account access and offer callback verification via registered email

privacyRules.maskedDataDisplay: Agents may only see last 4 digits of payment cards, last 4 of SSN. Full data is never visible.

dataSharing.rule: Account data may only be shared with the verified account owner or an authorized delegate on file
dataSharing.exceptions: Law enforcement with valid court order; fraud investigation team with case number
dataSharing.forbidden: Sharing account details with non-verified callers, family members not listed as delegates, or third parties

privacyRules.dataCorrection: Customers may request correction of contact info, billing address. SSN/name changes require identity documentation via store visit.
privacyRules.dataRetention: Agents must not delete or modify CRM notes; additions only
privacyRules.piiInCommunications: Agents must never read full PII (card numbers, SSN, DOB) aloud on a call. Confirm only masked versions.
privacyRules.screenSharing: If using assisted screen sharing session, agent must ensure no other customer data is visible

accountInformationHandling.beforeVerification: Only confirm that an account exists for the phone number or account number provided. No details.

canShare: Last payment date and masked amount
cannotShare: Call logs of other numbers on account
Internal credit score or churn risk score

accountInformationHandling.accountChangeLog: All changes made to account must be logged with timestamp, agent ID, and reason code
accountInformationHandling.disputedOwnership: If customer claims ownership but fails verification, do NOT assume ownership. Route to identity mismatch escalation flow.

timelineHandling.rule: Never state a timeline you cannot guarantee

approvedTimelines.creditProcessing: Approved credits post within 1-2 billing cycles
approvedTimelines.refundToCard: Refund to original payment method: 5-10 business days after approval
approvedTimelines.ticketResolution: Priority 1: 24 hours; Priority 2: 3-5 business days; Priority 3: 7-10 business days
approvedTimelines.portOut: 1-3 business days (not guaranteed; depends on gaining carrier)
approvedTimelines.simActivation: New SIM activation: typically 2-4 hours; may take up to 24 hours
approvedTimelines.numberReactivation: Suspended number reactivation: 1-4 hours after payment received

timelineHandling.disclaimerRequired: All timelines are estimates. Actual timelines may vary due to system processing, billing cycles, or third-party dependencies.
timelineHandling.forbidden: Stating exact clock times ('by 3pm today') or calendar dates ('by next Friday') unless system has confirmed this

refundHandling.frontlineAuthority: Up to $25 credit without supervisor approval (one-time, per billing cycle, per account)
refundHandling.aboveLimit: Requires written approval from Team Lead or Senior Manager

refundMethods.creditToAccount: Applied to next bill; most common method
refundMethods.originalPaymentMethod: Available for charges within 30 days; requires billing specialist
refundMethods.check: Only for closed accounts; 7-14 business days

notEligibleForRefund: Charges older than 90 days (unless fraud is confirmed)
International usage charges confirmed as used by account holder
Charges customer agreed to at time of service activation
refundHandling.refundRequestLogging: All refund requests must be logged in CRM regardless of approval status

compensationHandling.rule: Compensation is not automatic for any service disruption
eligibilityFactors: Whether outage was in covered area per SLA
Whether outage was NexaLink infrastructure vs. external (e.g., natural disaster)
Customer's service tier (business SLA vs consumer)
compensationHandling.approvalFlow: Compensation request → Billing Specialist review → Supervisor sign-off for >$50 → Credit applied
compensationHandling.maxFrontlineCompensation: $25 service credit (one-time per outage event, per account)
compensationHandling.businessCustomerSLA: Business customers with SLA agreements may be eligible for SLA credits per their contract terms; frontline must not calculate or promise SLA credits — route to Business Account Team
compensationHandling.forbidden: Offering compensation in cash, gift cards, or free devices. Compensation is account credit only.

triggerConditions: Customer requests plan downgrade to lowest tier
Customer has been a customer for 12+ months and is threatening to leave
retentionHandling.retentionOfferCatalog: Agents must use only offers in the current CRM retention catalog. Offers refresh quarterly.
frontlineRetentionOffers: Plan price hold for 1 billing cycle
Free add-on for 1 month (from approved list)
supervisorRetentionOffers: Plan discount for 3-6 months (from approved catalog)
retentionSpecialistOffers: Free premium add-on bundle for 6 months
retentionHandling.forbidden: Creating offers not in the retention catalog. Matching competitor pricing without authorization. Promising future promotions not confirmed.
retentionHandling.documentation: All retention offers presented, accepted, or declined must be documented in CRM retention workflow

policyCategories.title: Customer Identity Verification Policy
policyCategories.summary: Governs the process and requirements for verifying customer identity before any account information is disclosed or any account action is taken.
policyCategories.scope: All inbound contacts regardless of channel, including phone, chat, and social media direct messages.
supportedIssues: Standard identity verification for account access
Alternative verification when primary method fails
Verification for deceased account holder scenarios
unsupportedRequests: Bypassing verification for 'quick questions'
Verbal-only identity for account changes
Verification by callback number alone
Partial verification for sensitive changes
allowedActions: Perform standard two-factor identity check (PIN + SSN last 4 or PIN + billing ZIP)
Offer alternative verification path after one failed attempt
Lock account access after 2 consecutive failed verification attempts
Advise customer on how to reset PIN through self-service portal with email verification
Accept delegate authorization if delegate name is pre-registered in CRM
Request in-store ID verification for high-sensitivity changes
forbiddenPromises: Promising to bypass verification 'just this once'
Confirming account details before verification is complete
Accepting caller's claim of identity without completing the verification process
Promising that a failed verification will not be flagged

requiredVerification.standard: Account PIN (4-6 digits) + last 4 digits of SSN on file
requiredVerification.alternate1: Account PIN + billing ZIP code on file
requiredVerification.alternate2: Security question on file + 4 digits of SSN
requiredVerification.businessAccount: Business passcode + company EIN last 4 digits
requiredVerification.highSecurity: For SIM swap, port-out, or account owner change: Full SSN + PIN + verbal confirmation of last payment amount
requiredVerification.failureAction: After 2 failures: lock account inquiry access, offer account PIN reset via registered email only

standardHandlingFlow: Step 1: Welcome customer and confirm the phone number or account number they are calling about
Step 2: Inform customer that verification is required to access account details
Step 3: Request primary verification factors (PIN + SSN last 4 or PIN + billing ZIP)
Step 4: If verification succeeds, proceed with the call
Step 5: If first attempt fails, offer alternate verification path once
Step 6: If second attempt fails, lock account access and advise customer to visit store with government ID or reset PIN via email link sent to registered email address
Step 7: Log all verification attempts (pass/fail) in CRM with timestamp
requiredDataPoints: Calling number or account number stated by customer

policyBoundaries.noExceptions: Verification cannot be waived for any reason except law enforcement court order
policyBoundaries.delegateVerification: Delegates must be pre-registered; verbal permission from account owner during the same call is NOT sufficient to add a delegate
policyBoundaries.manualOverride: Supervisors may not override verification requirements; this is a hard compliance rule

escalationTriggers: Customer claims they are the account owner but fails verification twice
Customer claims someone else changed their PIN fraudulently
Customer is calling on behalf of deceased account holder without estate documentation
Multiple failed verification attempts on same account within 24 hours (fraud signal)
safeResponseGuidance: 'For your account security, I need to verify your identity before I can access any account details.'
'I understand this is frustrating, but these steps are in place to protect your account.'
'I was not able to verify your identity with the information provided. I can send a PIN reset link to your registered email if you'd like.'
'If you are unable to complete phone verification, you are welcome to visit any NexaLink store with a government-issued photo ID.'
prohibitedResponseExamples: 'I can tell from your voice that you're the account holder, so I'll just skip the verification this time.'
'Go ahead, what did you want to know about the account?' (before verification)
'Your neighbor called earlier and I verified the account then, so you're fine.'
'Just tell me the last 4 of your card number and that should be enough.'

approvedResponsePatterns.verificationRequest: Before I can access your account, I'll need to verify your identity. Could you please provide me with your 4-digit account PIN and the last 4 digits of the Social Security Number associated with this account?
approvedResponsePatterns.verificationFailed: I'm sorry, the information provided doesn't match what we have on file. I'd like to try one more verification method. Could you provide your account PIN along with the billing ZIP code on file?
approvedResponsePatterns.verificationLockedOut: For security reasons, I'm unable to process further verification attempts by phone at this time. I can send a secure PIN reset link to the email address registered on the account, or you are welcome to visit a NexaLink store with your government-issued photo ID. Which would you prefer?
approvedResponsePatterns.delegateScenario: I can see that your name is not listed as an authorized delegate on this account. I am not able to share account details without that authorization on file. The primary account holder can add you as an authorized delegate through the MyAccount app or at any store.

followUpRules: If account is locked, send automated PIN reset email to registered address within 5 minutes
Log all failed verification attempts with timestamp and method
If fraud is suspected (multiple failures, unusual access patterns), flag for Fraud team review
policyCategories.agentAuthorityLevel: FULL — Agent may complete verification independently without supervisor
supervisorAuthorityRequiredFor: Overriding a verification lock (NOT permitted even with supervisor — must go through self-service or store)
riskSignals: Customer cannot provide PIN or any alternate verification
Customer insists they 'don't have time' for verification
Calling number is different from registered number AND verification fails
Customer expresses unusual urgency about SIM or number-related changes
commonCustomerPhrases: Can you just look up my account?
I don't know my PIN
Why do I have to verify every time?
My wife handles the account
I need you to access my account right now
I forgot my security question

policyCategories.title: Billing Error and Duplicate Recharge Dispute Policy
policyCategories.summary: Covers handling of billing errors, unrecognized charges, duplicate payments or recharges, and incorrect billing amounts on both postpaid and prepaid accounts.
policyCategories.scope: All consumer and business accounts; both postpaid monthly bill disputes and prepaid recharge issues.
supportedIssues: Duplicate charge for same billing period
Manual recharge not reflected in balance
unsupportedRequests: Retroactive plan change to lower the bill for a past billing cycle already closed
Removing confirmed usage charges (data overage, roaming) that customer disputes as 'unauthorized' without fraud claim
Reversing third-party charges directly (must direct to third-party vendor)
Applying credits for issues older than 90 days without fraud investigation
allowedActions: Pull last 3 billing statements for review
Identify and explain individual line items on bill
Confirm plan change effective date and its billing impact
Apply a billing credit up to $25 without supervisor approval for clear errors
Submit billing dispute ticket for specialist review for amounts >$25
Provide third-party charge vendor contact information
Initiate BELO (Billing Error Line Open) investigation for systematic billing error
Confirm duplicate payment and initiate refund request to billing specialist
forbiddenPromises: Promising credit for charges that are confirmed as correct usage
Guaranteeing that a dispute will result in a credit
Promising that a third-party charge will be reversed without confirmation from that vendor
Stating a credit will be applied 'immediately' (credits process within 1-2 cycles)
Agreeing that the bill is 'wrong' without verifying the charge details in system
policyCategories.requiredVerification: Full identity verification (POL-001) must be completed before discussing any billing details.
standardHandlingFlow: Step 1: Complete identity verification
Step 2: Pull up billing details and allow customer to identify the specific charge(s) in question
Step 3: Review charge details in billing system — confirm charge type, date, and amount
Step 4: Check if charge is NexaLink-generated or third-party
Step 5a (NexaLink charge): Determine if charge is correct per plan terms or a billing error
Step 5b (Third-party charge): Advise customer to contact the third-party vendor; provide contact info if available
Step 6 (Error confirmed): Apply credit up to $25 (if within authority); escalate to billing specialist for >$25
Step 7 (Error not confirmed): Explain the charge, offer to email a bill explanation, and submit for specialist review if customer disagrees
Step 8: Log all details in CRM and provide ticket number
requiredDataPoints: Specific charge amount and date disputed
Customer's explanation of why charge is incorrect
System confirmation of charge type and origin

policyBoundaries.timeLimit: Billing disputes must be raised within 90 days of charge date for standard review; fraud exceptions extend to 12 months
policyBoundaries.creditLimit: Frontline credit authority: $25 maximum per interaction
policyBoundaries.thirdPartyCharges: NexaLink cannot reverse third-party charges; can flag to Block Third Party Billing on request
policyBoundaries.duplicateRecharge: If duplicate prepaid recharge is confirmed in system, credit to account balance; refund to payment method requires billing specialist

escalationTriggers: Dispute amount exceeds $100
Pattern of repeated billing errors on same account
Customer alleges deliberate fraud by NexaLink
Dispute involves active fraud investigation
Customer is a business account with SLA implications
safeResponseGuidance: 'I can see the charge on your account — let me pull up the details so I can explain exactly what it is for.'
'I understand this was unexpected. Let me review this with you and we'll figure out the right next step.'
'I've identified this as a duplicate charge. I'm applying a credit of $X to your account, which will reflect on your next billing statement.'
'For this particular amount, I'll need to submit a specialist review request, which typically takes 3-5 business days. You'll receive an update via email or phone.'
prohibitedResponseExamples: 'Your bill is definitely wrong, I'll just credit you the whole amount.' (without verification)
'I can refund that to your card right now.' (frontline cannot initiate card refunds)
'That third-party charge is our fault — we'll remove it.' (without investigation)
'I'll just put a note and someone will call you back.' (without ticket number or timeline)

approvedResponsePatterns.duplicateCharge: I can confirm that your account was charged twice for [amount] on [date]. This appears to be a processing error. I am applying a credit of [amount] to your account, which will appear on your next billing statement. Your confirmation reference is [ticket number].
approvedResponsePatterns.unrecognizedCharge: I can see that charge on your bill. It appears to be from a third-party service provider billed through your NexaLink account. I can provide you with their contact information so they can assist you with a refund. I can also block future third-party charges on your account — would you like me to do that?
approvedResponsePatterns.disputeEscalation: The amount you've disputed requires a review by our billing specialist team. I've submitted a case on your behalf and you can expect an update within 3-5 business days. Your case number is [ticket number]. Is there anything else I can help you with today?
approvedResponsePatterns.chargeIsCorrect: After reviewing the details on your account, this charge appears to be correctly applied based on your current plan and usage. I'd be happy to email you a detailed explanation of this charge if that would help. Would you like me to do that?

followUpRules: All billing disputes must be logged with specific charge details, date, and outcome
If credit is applied, log amount, reason code, and authorization level
If escalated, confirm with customer that they will receive follow-up within stated SLA
If third-party billing block is requested, log as account change
policyCategories.agentAuthorityLevel: PARTIAL — Can apply credit up to $25; must escalate amounts above $25 to billing specialist or supervisor
supervisorAuthorityRequiredFor: Overriding a charge that system shows as correct usage
Applying credit outside 90-day window for non-fraud cases
riskSignals: Pattern of monthly billing disputes from same account
Customer alleging NexaLink fraud
Multiple duplicate charges in short period
Customer mentions CFPB or state regulator
commonCustomerPhrases: I was charged twice
I don't recognize this charge
My bill is higher than it should be
I recharged but my balance didn't update
Why is there a $X charge on my bill?
I want a refund for this charge

policyCategories.title: Postpaid Account Billing Dispute Policy
policyCategories.summary: Specific policy for disputing line items, charges, and billing errors on postpaid monthly accounts including overage charges, plan billing, and device payment disputes.
policyCategories.scope: All postpaid consumer and business accounts.
supportedIssues: Upgrade fee or activation fee dispute
Prorated charge dispute after plan change
unsupportedRequests: Retroactively applying a plan change to reduce a past closed bill
Removing confirmed overage charges that match confirmed usage records
Removing device payment plan obligations
Adjusting charges for services customer voluntarily used
allowedActions: Access and review itemized bill details with customer
Explain proration calculations
Confirm plan terms against what was billed
Waive one-time late fee if account is in good standing and first offense
Apply credit up to $25 for confirmed billing errors without approval
Submit billing specialist review for amounts >$25
Explain AutoPay enrollment, failure reasons, and enrollment process
Remove paper bill fee if customer enrolls in paperless billing during call
forbiddenPromises: Promising to remove confirmed overage charges without supervisor approval
Agreeing to 'wipe' the current bill without formal approval
Promising a credit without confirming system shows the error
Guaranteeing no future overage charges without changing plan
Promising a billing correction will appear on current bill (may take to next cycle)
standardHandlingFlow: Step 1: Complete identity verification
Step 2: Access current and prior bill; ask customer to identify specific dispute
Step 3: Review line item in detail — confirm plan, usage data, device payment ledger
Step 4: Determine if charge is (a) billing error, (b) correct but customer is confused, or (c) unclear requiring specialist
Step 5a (billing error): Apply credit if within authority; escalate if above limit
Step 5b (correct charge, customer confused): Explain calmly and offer written explanation via email
Step 5c (unclear): Submit billing specialist review; provide ticket number and timeline
Step 6: If customer disputes overage charges as unauthorized, offer to add plan feature to prevent future overages
Step 7: Confirm resolution or next steps with customer; document in CRM
requiredDataPoints: Customer's claim about what was expected
System record of plan and usage

policyBoundaries.lateFeWaiver: One late fee waiver per rolling 12 months per account, first-time offense only
policyBoundaries.overageDispute: Confirmed overage charges require supervisor approval to waive; agents cannot unilaterally waive usage charges
policyBoundaries.dpDispute: Device Payment Plan disputes require billing specialist — cannot be resolved at frontline
policyBoundaries.taxAndSurcharges: Government-mandated taxes and fees are non-waivable; regulatory recovery fees may be waivable with supervisor approval

escalationTriggers: Dispute amount >$100
Customer claims to have never agreed to a device payment plan
Customer claims identity fraud led to unauthorized plan upgrade or device purchase
Business account with invoice over $500 in dispute
Repeated dispute of same charge across multiple calls
safeResponseGuidance: 'Let me walk you through each charge so you can see exactly what the bill reflects for this cycle.'
'This charge here is your data overage — you used X GB over your plan limit. I can show you how to avoid that in the future if you'd like.'
'I can see that your AutoPay did not process this month, which resulted in a late fee. Since this is the first time this has happened, I'm able to waive that fee for you.'
prohibitedResponseExamples: 'Those overage charges are ridiculous — I agree with you, let me just remove them.' (without authorization)
'I'll just credit your whole bill for this month.' (above authority)
'That device payment is wrong, I'll cancel it.' (cannot cancel DPP at frontline)

approvedResponsePatterns.lateFeWaiver: I can see this is the first time a late payment fee has appeared on your account. As a courtesy, I've waived the $X late fee this time. To avoid this in the future, I'd be happy to set up AutoPay for you — would that be helpful?
approvedResponsePatterns.overageExplain: Your data overage charge of $X reflects [Y GB] used beyond your [plan name] plan's [Z GB] limit. Would you like me to add an unlimited data add-on to prevent overages going forward? I can also review plan upgrade options if you're regularly going over.
approvedResponsePatterns.errorCredit: I've confirmed that this charge was applied in error. I've credited $X to your account, and this will appear on your next bill. Your case reference number is [number].
approvedResponsePatterns.dpDispute: Disputes about your Device Payment Plan require a review by our billing specialist team. I've opened a case for you — case number [number] — and you can expect an update within 3-5 business days.

followUpRules: Log charge details, customer's claim, agent determination, and action taken
If credit applied, note amount and authorization level
For escalated disputes, confirm customer has case number and knows response timeline
policyCategories.agentAuthorityLevel: PARTIAL — Standard dispute handling; credit up to $25; waive late fee (first offense only)
supervisorAuthorityRequiredFor: Any billing exception not covered in policy
riskSignals: Multiple billing disputes in 3 months
Customer mentions attorney or FCC
Dispute amount is very large relative to plan cost
Customer claims NexaLink changed their plan without consent
commonCustomerPhrases: My bill is higher than last month
I didn't use that much data
I never agreed to this charge
Why is my device payment different?
I thought AutoPay would cover it
My plan should be $X a month

policyCategories.title: Prepaid Recharge and Top-Up Failure Policy
policyCategories.summary: Handles cases where a prepaid recharge did not reflect in account balance, recharge failed at payment, double recharge occurred, or recharge voucher/PIN did not work.
policyCategories.scope: All prepaid consumer accounts.
supportedIssues: Payment processed but balance not updated
Voucher/PIN code rejected or shows as already used
AutoRecharge failed to trigger on low balance
Recharge via third-party retailer not applied
Recharge applied but plan not activated
unsupportedRequests: Applying credit for recharge that has no payment confirmation
Crediting a third-party retailer recharge without a receipt or transaction ID
Overriding voucher expiry for expired PIN codes
Refunding recharged balance back to cash for prepaid accounts (prepaid is generally non-refundable)
allowedActions: Look up recharge transaction history in billing system
Confirm whether recharge was received and processed
Manually trigger balance refresh if system shows recharge was processed but not reflected
Credit equivalent balance if system confirms charge but no balance update (processing error)
Submit investigation ticket for third-party retailer recharge claims with receipt
Block AutoRecharge feature and re-set up if malfunctioning
Report voucher PIN as defective and credit equivalent if PIN was issued by NexaLink
forbiddenPromises: Crediting balance without confirming payment processing in system
Promising a credit for retailer recharge without transaction evidence
Promising exact time balance will update after manual credit
Guaranteeing refund to credit card for prepaid recharge (prepaid is non-refundable per T&C)
policyCategories.requiredVerification: Full identity verification (POL-001) required before any balance or transaction access
standardHandlingFlow: Step 1: Complete identity verification
Step 2: Ask customer for recharge details — amount, date/time, method (app, web, voucher, retailer)
Step 3: Pull recharge transaction history in CRM
Step 4a (recharge confirmed in system, balance not updated): Trigger manual balance refresh; apply credit if system confirms payment received
Step 4b (recharge shows as failed): Confirm no charge occurred; advise customer to retry payment
Step 4c (recharge via retailer): Request receipt or transaction ID; submit investigation ticket
Step 4d (voucher issue): Verify voucher PIN in system; if NexaLink-issued and defective, apply equivalent credit
Step 5: Confirm updated balance with customer
Step 6: Log outcome in CRM with transaction reference
requiredDataPoints: Transaction ID or receipt if available

policyBoundaries.prepaidRefundPolicy: Prepaid top-up amounts are generally non-refundable per Terms and Conditions. Exceptions for confirmed processing errors require billing specialist approval.
policyBoundaries.retailerRecharge: NexaLink cannot confirm retailer-side transactions without a transaction ID or receipt. Investigation takes 3-5 business days.
policyBoundaries.voucherPolicy: Expired NexaLink vouchers are non-creditable. Lost or stolen vouchers are not eligible for replacement. Third-party vouchers (e.g., from retailers) must be addressed with that retailer.
policyBoundaries.doubleRecharge: If double recharge is confirmed, second recharge amount is credited to account balance — not refunded to payment method

escalationTriggers: Customer claims large recharge amount ($50+) is lost without confirmation
Multiple failed recharges in short period suggesting payment method issue
Customer alleges fraudulent recharge on their account by a third party
System shows payment processed but balance consistently not updating (possible system bug)
safeResponseGuidance: 'Let me check our system for the transaction you're describing. I'll need the approximate time and the amount you recharged.'
'I can see your payment was received on our end but the balance update seems to have been delayed. Let me apply a manual refresh now.'
'For recharges made at a retail store, I'll need to open an investigation. Please keep your receipt — you'll need the transaction ID. This typically takes 3-5 business days.'
prohibitedResponseExamples: 'Just try recharging again — you'll be fine.' (if original charge was processed, double charge risk)
'That retailer is not our problem.' (must assist in investigation process)
'I'll refund that to your credit card.' (prepaid refunds are restricted)

approvedResponsePatterns.systemConfirmedError: I can confirm that your payment of $[amount] was received on [date] but your balance was not updated due to a processing delay. I've applied a manual credit of $[amount] to your account. Your balance should now reflect [updated balance].
approvedResponsePatterns.noPaymentRecord: I've reviewed your account and I'm not seeing a record of that transaction in our system. This may mean the payment did not complete. Could you check your payment method's statement for a pending or completed charge? If you do see a charge, please provide me with the transaction reference and I'll open an investigation right away.
approvedResponsePatterns.retailerInvestigation: For purchases made at a third-party retailer, I'll need to open an investigation. Your case number is [number]. If you can share your receipt or the transaction ID from the retailer, that will help us resolve this faster. You can expect an update within 3-5 business days.

followUpRules: If investigation opened, call back or email customer within 5 business days with update
If manual credit applied, log transaction reference and reason code
If retailer investigation is pending, document receipt status in CRM
policyCategories.agentAuthorityLevel: PARTIAL — Can apply balance credit for confirmed system errors; retailer disputes must be escalated to billing specialist
supervisorAuthorityRequiredFor: Overriding prepaid non-refundable policy for exceptional cases
riskSignals: Multiple recharge claims without payment confirmation
Customer cannot provide any transaction evidence
Pattern of recharge disputes from same account
commonCustomerPhrases: I recharged but my balance didn't change
My voucher PIN says already used
I paid but have no service
The app took my money but balance is zero
My auto-recharge didn't trigger

policyCategories.title: Refund and Service Compensation Policy
policyCategories.summary: Defines eligibility, approval levels, methods, and restrictions for refunds and service credits/compensation to customers.
policyCategories.scope: All consumer and business accounts; all service types.
supportedIssues: Refund for unused prepaid balance on account closure (limited)
SLA breach credit for business accounts
unsupportedRequests: Cash refunds (NexaLink does not issue cash)
Refund to a different payment method than the original
Refund for charges the customer agreed to but later regrets
Refund for competitor service interruptions
Retroactive compensation for issues not reported at the time
Compensation in the form of free devices or gift cards
allowedActions: Apply account credit up to $25 for frontline agents
Submit refund request to billing specialist for amounts $25-$250
Confirm refund status for previously submitted requests
Explain refund timeline and method
Apply automatic SLA credit for business accounts (if SLA terms confirm eligibility)
Submit outage compensation request (per POL-027)
forbiddenPromises: Guaranteeing refund approval before specialist review
Promising refund within specific number of days without billing system confirmation
Promising refund to a non-original payment method
Promising any form of compensation not in the approved policy catalog
standardHandlingFlow: Step 1: Verify identity
Step 2: Confirm the specific charge or overpayment being claimed
Step 3: Validate in system whether the refund is clearly due (confirmed error, duplicate, etc.)
Step 4: If confirmed and within frontline authority ($25), apply credit immediately
Step 5: If >$25 or requires policy review, submit billing specialist request with full details
Step 6: Inform customer of timeline and case reference number
Step 7: Log in CRM with reason code, amount, and authorization level
requiredDataPoints: Amount and basis for refund claim

refundMethods.accountCredit: Default method; applied within 1-2 billing cycles
refundMethods.cardRefund: Available for duplicate payments within 30 days; requires billing specialist; 5-10 business days
refundMethods.checkRefund: For closed accounts only; 7-14 business days

compensationLimits.aboveLimit: Requires Team Lead or Senior Manager written approval

policyBoundaries.outageCompensation: Must follow POL-027 — not automatic; duration and impact must be verified
policyBoundaries.goodwillCredit: Goodwill credits are limited to once per 6 months per account
policyBoundaries.slaCredit: Business SLA credits are calculated per the customer's contract — frontline must not calculate or promise; must route to Business Account Team

escalationTriggers: Refund amount >$100
Customer disputes refund was received but system shows it was processed
Customer demands cash or alternate payment method refund
Business account SLA compensation claim
safeResponseGuidance: 'I've confirmed the error and I've applied a $X credit to your account. You'll see this on your next billing statement.'
'For a refund of this amount, I'll need to involve our billing specialist team. I've opened a case — case number [X] — and you can expect an update within 3-5 business days.'
'I understand your frustration and I want to make this right. While I can apply a courtesy credit today, for the full amount you're requesting, I'll need to escalate this for review.'
prohibitedResponseExamples: 'I'll refund everything — don't worry about it.' (above authority)
'Your refund will be in your account by tomorrow.' (cannot guarantee timeline)
'We'll send you a check.' (not applicable for active accounts)
'I'll match whatever [competitor] offered you.' (not a valid refund basis)

approvedResponsePatterns.frontlineCredit: I've applied a credit of $[amount] to your account for [reason]. This will appear on your next billing statement. Your reference number is [number].
approvedResponsePatterns.escalatedRefund: For a refund of $[amount], I need to route this to our billing specialist team for review and approval. I've created a case — number [X] — and you'll receive an update within 3-5 business days. The refund, if approved, would be applied to your account within 1-2 billing cycles.
approvedResponsePatterns.refundStatus: I can see that a refund of $[amount] was approved on [date] and [was applied to your account / is being processed for return to your [payment method]]. Estimated completion is [timeline].

followUpRules: Log all credits and refund requests with case number, amount, method, and authority level
If escalated, confirm follow-up timeline and owner
If refund to card was promised, verify with billing specialist that card refund process was initiated
policyCategories.agentAuthorityLevel: PARTIAL — Credit up to $25 immediately; all refunds above this level require escalation
riskSignals: Frequent refund requests from same account
Refund claim with no supporting evidence in system
Customer threatening legal action over refund denial
commonCustomerPhrases: I want my money back
I need a refund
You owe me a credit
I want compensation for the outage
This was charged in error, please refund
When will my refund come?

policyCategories.title: Network Outage and Service Disruption Handling Policy
policyCategories.summary: Defines how agents and the AI system should handle reports of network outages, service disruptions, and loss of connectivity. Covers what can and cannot be promised regarding restoration and compensation.
policyCategories.scope: All consumer and business mobile, broadband, and fixed wireless service accounts.
supportedIssues: Complete loss of mobile service in an area
Data service not working while voice works
Known outage — customer calling for status update
Suspected outage based on customer report
Broadband or fixed wireless service outage
Business service disruption with SLA implications
unsupportedRequests: Guaranteeing service restoration by a specific time without official ETR from NOC
Confirming or denying the cause of an outage without official NOC communication
Promising automatic compensation without following outage compensation policy
Redirecting 911 calls — always connect emergency calls immediately
allowedActions: Check NOC outage dashboard for active incidents in customer's area
Confirm whether a known outage exists for the customer's area
Log a new service disruption report if no outage is found in system
Provide ETR from NOC bulletin if available
Offer troubleshooting steps for individual connectivity issues (not outage-related)
Submit network trouble ticket for investigation
Advise customer to call 911 via alternate means if emergency services access is affected
Initiate outage compensation review request per POL-027
forbiddenPromises: Promising service will be restored by a specific time without confirmed ETR
Stating the cause of the outage (equipment failure, fiber cut, etc.) without official NOC confirmation
Promising automatic bill credit for all outages
Claiming the outage is fixed before receiving official NOC all-clear
Telling customer that the issue is only on their device if outage is confirmed in area
policyCategories.requiredVerification: Full identity verification for account-specific issues; outage status check can be done without full verification
standardHandlingFlow: Step 1: Acknowledge service issue and express empathy
Step 2: Verify identity (if account-specific action needed)
Step 3: Perform location check — ask for ZIP code or cross-street reference
Step 4: Check NOC outage dashboard for active incidents in customer's area
Step 5a (outage confirmed): Inform customer of confirmed outage; provide ETR if available; log that customer was affected
Step 5b (no outage found): Proceed with individual troubleshooting (device, SIM, settings)
Step 5c (outage unclear): Log service disruption report; escalate to technical specialist if individual troubleshooting fails
Step 6: For business accounts — check SLA terms and route to Business Account Team if SLA breach threshold is being approached
Step 7: Advise customer on interim options (Wi-Fi calling, alternate device)
Step 8: Offer to follow up when service is restored (proactive callback/SMS notification)
requiredDataPoints: Service type affected (voice, data, text, all)
Whether other devices in area are also affected

policyBoundaries.etrlPolicy: ETR (Estimated Time to Restore) can only be communicated if it has been officially published by the NOC for that incident
policyBoundaries.causeDisclosure: Outage cause information is internal; agents must not speculate about cause
policyBoundaries.compensationLink: Outage compensation is handled under POL-027; not automatic and requires separate review
policyBoundaries.emergencyServices: If customer states they cannot reach 911, this is CRITICAL — immediately advise alternate emergency access methods and log as P1 incident
policyBoundaries.naturalDisaster: During federally declared disasters, NexaLink activates emergency protocols; agents will receive a briefing via NOC bulletin. Service credits are not guaranteed during force majeure events.

escalationTriggers: Customer cannot reach emergency services (911) — P1 immediate escalation
Outage has been active for more than 4 hours without ETR
Business customer with SLA implications
Multiple customers reporting same new area issue (possible unreported outage — escalate to NOC)
Customer reports medical dependency on service
safeResponseGuidance: 'I'm sorry to hear you're experiencing a service disruption. Let me check if there are any known issues in your area.'
'I can confirm there is a network issue affecting your area. Our technical teams are actively working to restore service. At this time, we do not have a confirmed restoration time.'
'I don't see an active outage reported in your area. Let me walk you through a few troubleshooting steps that often resolve this type of issue.'

'I've logged your service disruption report so our team can investigate. If this turns out to be a broader area issue, it will be added to our outage tracking. Is there a number I can reach you at when service is restored?'
prohibitedResponseExamples: 'Service should be back up by 3pm today.' (without confirmed ETR)
'The outage is caused by a fiber cut on [road].' (internal information not for disclosure)
'You'll automatically receive a credit for this outage.' (not guaranteed)
'It sounds like it's just your phone — the network is fine.' (if not confirmed by diagnostics)
'I have no idea why service is down — could be anything.' (vague and unhelpful)

approvedResponsePatterns.confirmedOutage: I can confirm that there is a network service disruption affecting your area [ZIP/city]. Our engineering team is actively working to restore service. At this time, I do not have a confirmed estimated time of restoration. I've noted your account as affected. Would you like me to send you an SMS notification when service is restored?
approvedResponsePatterns.noOutageFound: I'm not seeing any active outage reports for your area. Let's walk through a few steps to see if we can get your service working. First, could you try restarting your device? [Follow troubleshooting flow]
approvedResponsePatterns.outageWithETR: There is a known service disruption in your area, and our team is working toward a restoration target of approximately [ETR]. Please note this is an estimate and may be updated. I've added a note to your account. Is there anything else I can help you with in the meantime?
approvedResponsePatterns.emergencyAccessCritical: I understand you cannot reach emergency services. This is very important. Please try calling 911 from a landline or a different phone immediately. I am logging this as a critical service issue. If there is an active emergency, please do not wait on this call.

followUpRules: Log all outage reports in CRM with location, service type, time reported
If customer is affected by confirmed outage, tag account as 'OUTAGE_AFFECTED' for potential compensation review
If business customer's SLA is being impacted, immediately route to Business Account Team
If 911 access issue reported, escalate to P1 NOC ticket immediately
policyCategories.agentAuthorityLevel: PARTIAL — Can confirm known outages, log new disruption reports, provide ETR from NOC; cannot promise compensation or restoration time
supervisorAuthorityRequiredFor: Immediate service credit for business SLA breach
Approving any compensation commitment for outage
riskSignals: Customer reports 911 access failure
Outage duration exceeds SLA threshold
Multiple customers calling about same unreported area
Business account with critical operations affected
commonCustomerPhrases: I have no service
The network is down in my area
My internet has been out for hours
When will service come back?
Is there an outage?
My 4G is not working
I can't make calls

policyCategories.title: Weak Signal, Poor Call Quality, and Slow Data Speed Policy
policyCategories.summary: Covers handling of ongoing or intermittent signal strength issues, poor call quality, dropped calls, and slow mobile data speeds that are not part of a confirmed outage.
policyCategories.scope: All mobile and broadband consumer and business accounts.
supportedIssues: Persistent weak signal at a specific location
Slow data speeds below advertised levels
Poor voice call quality or echo
Wi-Fi calling not working as expected
Roaming signal weak or not connecting
unsupportedRequests: Guaranteeing signal strength at a specific address
Promising speed improvements without network upgrade
Agreeing that NexaLink is at fault without technical investigation
Promising a refund solely based on speed complaints without diagnostics
allowedActions: Check coverage map for customer's reported location
Perform remote SIM and network diagnostics
Guide customer through device troubleshooting steps
Submit a network improvement ticket (NIT) for weak signal area
Check if customer's plan has a speed threshold or throttling policy
Verify if customer's data allotment has been exhausted (causing speed reduction)
Offer Wi-Fi Calling enablement as interim workaround
Check if network congestion is affecting area during peak hours
Advise on device APN settings if misconfigured
forbiddenPromises: Guaranteeing signal improvement at a specific address
Promising a network tower will be built or upgraded
Agreeing to provide refund solely because speeds are not 'fast enough' without diagnostics
Promising speed test results will improve by a specific date
policyCategories.requiredVerification: Full identity verification for account-specific actions; technical questions can be addressed generally before verification
standardHandlingFlow: Step 1: Acknowledge the signal or speed issue with empathy
Step 2: Gather information — location, device model, when started, frequency (always or sometimes)
Step 3: Check NOC dashboard for outage or congestion notice in area
Step 4: Check coverage map for reported location
Step 5: Check account for throttling status (deprioritized, data exhausted, etc.)
Step 6: Perform device diagnostics — restart, SIM re-seat, APN check
Step 7: If issue persists after troubleshooting, submit Network Improvement Ticket (NIT)
Step 8: Offer workaround (Wi-Fi calling, data booster add-on, signal booster device)
Step 9: Log all findings in CRM and give customer ticket reference

policyBoundaries.coverageDisclaimer: Coverage maps are approximate; actual coverage may vary based on terrain, buildings, and device capabilities
policyBoundaries.throttlingDisclosure: If customer's data has been throttled due to plan limits, agent must disclose this clearly and explain the plan's terms
policyBoundaries.speedGuarantee: NexaLink does not guarantee specific speeds under consumer plans; speeds are 'up to' maximums. Business plans may have minimum speed SLAs.
policyBoundaries.homeCoverage: If customer's home/office has persistently poor coverage, agent may offer a free signal booster evaluation (where program is active)
policyBoundaries.networkImprovementTicket: NIT submissions are routed to the network planning team; they do not result in immediate action and timelines are not predictable

escalationTriggers: Issue has been reported 3+ times without resolution
Business customer with operations impacted by persistent signal issue
Customer is threatening to cancel due to signal issue
Coverage map shows good coverage but issue persists after full troubleshooting
safeResponseGuidance: 'I understand having poor signal is really frustrating. Let me pull up our coverage information for your area and we'll see what's happening.'

'I can see your data for this cycle has been used up, which means your speeds have been reduced to [X Mbps] as per your plan. Adding a data booster would restore full speeds — would you like to do that?'
'I've submitted a network improvement request for your area. While this won't fix the issue immediately, it does go to our network planning team for evaluation.'
prohibitedResponseExamples: 'NexaLink guarantees 5G signal everywhere.' (false claim)
'I'll give you a refund for every month your signal has been bad.' (above authority, not automatic)
'A new tower will be built in your area soon.' (speculation)
'Your phone is probably just bad.' (dismissive without diagnostics)

approvedResponsePatterns.throttlingDisclosed: I can see that you've used your full [X GB] data allocation for this billing cycle. Your speeds have been reduced to [Y Mbps] as a result, which is standard under your plan. Your speeds will return to normal on [next cycle date]. Would you like to add a Data Booster to restore full speeds today?
approvedResponsePatterns.coverageIssue: Our coverage map does show reduced coverage in that area, which may be contributing to the signal issues you're experiencing. I've submitted a network improvement request on your behalf. In the meantime, enabling Wi-Fi Calling may help with calls and texts when you're in that area. Would you like me to walk you through enabling that?
approvedResponsePatterns.troubleshootingComplete: We've completed the troubleshooting steps and the issue was not resolved. I've opened a technical ticket — number [X] — for our network team to investigate further. You can expect an update within 3-5 business days. Would you like me to set up a callback?

followUpRules: Log all troubleshooting steps taken and results
If NIT submitted, log ticket number in CRM
If customer is churn-risk due to signal issue, flag for retention team
Follow up within 5 business days if issue remains unresolved
policyCategories.agentAuthorityLevel: PARTIAL — Can perform troubleshooting and submit NIT; cannot promise resolution or compensation
supervisorAuthorityRequiredFor: Service credit for persistent signal issues
riskSignals: Issue reported 3+ times
Customer mentions cancellation or port-out
Business operations affected
Coverage map conflict with customer experience
commonCustomerPhrases: I barely get one bar at home
My data is really slow
My calls keep dropping
5G doesn't work here
Why is my internet so slow?
I'm being throttled for no reason

policyCategories.title: SIM Activation, Replacement, and KYC Verification Policy
policyCategories.summary: Covers new SIM activation, replacement SIM issuance, SIM swap requests, and Know Your Customer (KYC) identity document verification requirements.
policyCategories.scope: All consumer and business accounts requiring SIM actions.
supportedIssues: New SIM activation for new customer
Replacement SIM for lost or damaged SIM
KYC document submission and verification status
SIM blocked due to failed KYC
unsupportedRequests: SIM swap without completing high-security verification
Remote SIM replacement for fraud-flagged accounts without additional security steps
Bypassing KYC requirements for regulatory compliance reasons
Activating a SIM for a third party who is not the account holder without proper authorization
allowedActions: Initiate SIM activation for verified account holder
Initiate eSIM provisioning via email to registered address
Check KYC document submission status
Advise on required KYC documents
Escalate KYC document rejection to KYC specialist team
Replace SIM for verified account holder after completing high-security verification
Block a reported lost or stolen SIM immediately
Transfer number to replacement SIM
forbiddenPromises: Guaranteeing activation will complete within a specific clock time
Bypassing KYC for any reason
Performing SIM swap without completing enhanced identity verification
Promising that a rejected KYC document will be accepted on resubmission

requiredVerification.simSwapOrReplacement: HIGH SECURITY verification — Full SSN + PIN + last payment amount + verbal confirmation
requiredVerification.kycSubmission: Government-issued photo ID + proof of address (within 90 days)
requiredVerification.fraudFlag: If fraud flag is on account, do not process SIM swap; route to Fraud team (POL-017)

standardHandlingFlow: Step 1: Complete identity verification (enhanced for SIM swap)
Step 2: Check account for fraud flags or security holds before proceeding
Step 3: Confirm the SIM action requested and reason
Step 4: For SIM replacement: block old SIM, provision new SIM, initiate number transfer
Step 5: For eSIM: send provisioning QR code to registered email; guide activation
Step 6: For KYC issue: check document status; advise on acceptable documents; escalate if needed
Step 7: Confirm activation or processing timeline with customer
Step 8: Log all SIM actions in CRM with reason code
requiredDataPoints: SIM ICCID (current and new if applicable)
KYC document type and submission status

policyBoundaries.simSwapSecurity: SIM swap is a high-fraud-risk action — mandatory enhanced security verification; any doubt triggers fraud escalation
policyBoundaries.kycRegulatory: KYC is a regulatory requirement; agents cannot bypass or waive it
policyBoundaries.simActivationTime: SIM activation typically takes 2-4 hours; eSIM provisioning is near-instant after scan; do not guarantee specific times
policyBoundaries.lostSimReporting: Reporting a SIM as lost/stolen blocks it immediately; customer must request replacement SIM
policyBoundaries.fraudFlaggedAccount: If account has an active fraud flag, SIM swap cannot be processed by frontline — must go to Fraud team

escalationTriggers: SIM swap request on fraud-flagged account
SIM swap request from caller who cannot complete enhanced verification
Multiple failed KYC submissions
Customer suspects unauthorized SIM swap has already occurred (SIM swap fraud)
safeResponseGuidance: 'To protect your account, SIM replacements require a slightly more thorough identity check. I'll walk you through that now.'
'I've blocked your lost SIM immediately — your number is safe. Let me help you get a replacement SIM set up.'
'Your KYC document submission is currently under review. Reviews take 1-3 business days. I've noted your account.'
prohibitedResponseExamples: 'I'll just skip the extra verification since you've passed the basic check.' (not permitted for SIM swap)
'Your SIM will be active within an hour.' (cannot guarantee)
'KYC is just a formality — don't worry about it.' (minimizing regulatory requirement)

approvedResponsePatterns.simBlock: I've blocked your lost SIM immediately. Your phone number is secure. I can now help you arrange a replacement SIM. Would you prefer a physical SIM sent to your address or would you like to activate an eSIM on your device right now?
approvedResponsePatterns.esimActivation: I've sent the eSIM provisioning details to your registered email address. Once you receive the QR code, scan it in your device's Settings under 'Mobile Data' or 'Cellular.' If you don't receive the email within 15 minutes, please check your spam folder and let us know.
approvedResponsePatterns.kycStatus: Your identity documents are currently under review by our verification team. This typically takes 1-3 business days. Once approved, your service will be fully active. If there is a specific issue with your documents, you'll receive an email with details on how to resubmit.

followUpRules: Log all SIM actions with ICCID, date, reason, and agent
If SIM swap was processed, send security notification to registered email and primary contact number
If fraud suspected during SIM action request, log incident report and route to Fraud team
policyCategories.agentAuthorityLevel: PARTIAL — Can process standard SIM actions after verification; cannot process SIM swap on fraud-flagged accounts
supervisorAuthorityRequiredFor: Processing SIM action on accounts with security flags
riskSignals: Caller cannot complete enhanced verification for SIM swap
Request to change SIM and then immediately change registered email/number
Account shows recent PIN change before SIM swap request
commonCustomerPhrases: I need a new SIM
My SIM is damaged
How do I activate my eSIM?
My KYC was rejected
I lost my phone and need a new SIM
SIM swap for new device

policyCategories.title: Plan Change, Upgrade, and Downgrade Policy
policyCategories.summary: Governs how plan changes, upgrades, and downgrades are handled, including proration, eligibility, and restrictions.
policyCategories.scope: All postpaid and prepaid consumer and business accounts.
supportedIssues: Change from individual to family plan
unsupportedRequests: Retroactive plan change for a billing cycle already closed
Creating a custom plan not in the product catalog
Applying a plan change price from a historical promotion that is no longer active
Plan change that circumvents device payment plan contractual obligations
allowedActions: Review and explain current plan features vs requested plan
Process plan change effective immediately or at next billing cycle
Explain proration impact of mid-cycle plan change
Check plan change eligibility (device payment plan compatibility, contract terms)
Apply plan change from the approved product catalog
Offer relevant plan options based on customer's usage patterns
forbiddenPromises: Applying a plan price from an expired promotion
Promising that a plan change will not affect existing device payment plan terms
Guaranteeing the same promotional price when changing plans
Promising grandfathered pricing will be maintained after a voluntary plan change
standardHandlingFlow: Step 1: Verify identity
Step 2: Review current plan and what customer is requesting
Step 3: Check eligibility for requested plan change
Step 4: Explain proration impact if mid-cycle change
Step 5: Disclose any features or promotions lost upon plan change
Step 6: Confirm plan change with customer verbally
Step 7: Process plan change in system
Step 8: Provide confirmation and effective date

policyBoundaries.grandfatheredPlans: If customer is on a grandfathered plan, they must be clearly warned that downgrading or changing away from it means they cannot return to it
policyBoundaries.prorationPolicy: Plan changes mid-cycle are prorated; agents must explain the proration calculation before processing
policyBoundaries.deviceCompatibility: Some plans require compatible devices; agent must confirm device compatibility before processing upgrade
policyBoundaries.businessPlans: Business plan changes require account manager review for accounts with >10 lines

escalationTriggers: Customer wants to change plan but has disputed charges pending
Business account plan change requiring contract amendment
Customer disputes proration calculation after plan change
safeResponseGuidance: 'Before I make this change, I want to make sure you're aware of a few things — including how it may affect your current bill and any features you currently have.'
'I can see you're on our legacy [plan name] plan. If you switch away from this plan, you would not be able to return to it if it's been discontinued. Are you sure you'd like to proceed?'

policyCategories.title: Repeat Complaint and Reopened Ticket Policy
policyCategories.summary: Governs handling when a customer is calling back about an issue that was previously reported but not resolved, or when a resolved ticket needs to be reopened.
policyCategories.scope: All consumer and business accounts with prior complaint or ticket history.
supportedIssues: Customer reporting same issue for second or third time

Customer following up on a pending ticket
Previous agent made a commitment not honored
Escalating a stale or ignored ticket
unsupportedRequests: Reopening a ticket closed more than 180 days ago without management review

Create a new linked ticket as continuation of prior case

Agreeing that a previous agent was 'wrong' or 'lied' in a way that creates legal liability
Promising additional compensation beyond what is policy-approved based solely on repetition

Step 2: Pull up prior ticket history — read all notes from previous interactions
Step 3: Ask customer to describe the current status of the issue
Step 4: Cross-reference customer's report with prior ticket resolution notes
Step 5a (if resolution was ineffective): Reopen or create linked ticket; escalate priority
Step 5b (if resolution claimed but not confirmed): Escalate to supervisor; customer should not be told the system said it was fixed if it is not
Step 6: If 3+ repeat complaints: mandatory supervisor escalation
Step 7: Acknowledge prior agent commitment if documented; escalate to supervisor if undocumented commitment is claimed
Step 8: Provide new ticket reference and realistic timeline
requiredDataPoints: Number of prior complaints on same issue

Account opened in customer's name without consent
Suspicious login activity on MyAccount app

Sharing details of the fraud investigation with anyone other than the verified account owner
allowedActions: Accept and log fraud report from customer
Immediately freeze account if fraud is confirmed or strongly suspected
Block a reported SIM swap immediately
Initiate account security review
Transfer to Fraud Investigation Team

Advise customer to file a police report for identity theft

Promising all fraudulent charges will be reversed without investigation

Step 2: Attempt identity verification; if fails, still create fraud case
Step 3: If account takeover suspected: lock account access immediately
Step 4: Block any affected SIM or recently changed number
Step 5: Create fraud case in CRM with all details provided by customer
Step 6: Transfer to Fraud Investigation Team
Step 7: Advise customer on steps: change passwords, file police report, check credit report
Step 8: Provide fraud case number and Fraud team contact information
requiredDataPoints: Timeline of when unauthorized activity was first noticed
What specific actions or changes are unrecognized
Whether customer has changed passwords recently

policyBoundaries.accountFreezeAuthority: Frontline agents can initiate an account security hold; full investigation is handled by Fraud team
