policyBoundaries.lawEnforcement: NexaLink cooperates with law enforcement under valid legal process; customers should also file a police report for identity theft

escalationTriggers: ANY report of unauthorized SIM swap — immediate Fraud escalation
Customer reports account was opened without their knowledge
Multiple fraud indicators in one account (recent SIM swap + email change + large purchase)
Customer reports being extorted or threatened related to account
safeResponseGuidance: 'I take this very seriously and I'm going to make sure this is addressed right away. Let me put a security hold on your account immediately while I gather details.'

'I've created a fraud case — your case number is [X]. Our Fraud Investigation Team will contact you within [timeline] to walk through the next steps. In the meantime, I strongly recommend changing all passwords associated with this account.'
prohibitedResponseExamples: 'Are you sure it wasn't you? Maybe you forgot.' (dismissive and harmful)
'I can't do anything until the fraud team confirms it.' (frontline must still take protective action)
'All the charges will definitely be reversed.' (cannot promise without investigation)

approvedResponsePatterns.fraudReport: I'm very sorry to hear that. I'm taking immediate action. I've placed a security hold on your account and blocked the affected SIM. Your fraud case number is [X]. Our Fraud Investigation Team will reach you at [number] within 24-48 business hours. In the meantime, I recommend changing your NexaLink password and reviewing your credit report for any other unauthorized activity. Do you need guidance on how to file a police report as well?

followUpRules: All fraud cases must be logged in CRM and flagged for Fraud team within 1 hour
Account security hold must be confirmed before call ends
policyCategories.agentAuthorityLevel: PARTIAL — Can place security hold and log fraud case; investigation is Fraud team's responsibility
supervisorAuthorityRequiredFor: Clearing an active fraud hold without Fraud team sign-off
riskSignals: Recent SIM swap + account changes
Customer unfamiliar with charges after verification passes
Verification info recently changed on account
commonCustomerPhrases: Someone hacked my account
I didn't authorize this
Someone swapped my SIM
My number was ported without my permission
There are charges I didn't make
My account was stolen

policyCategories.title: Identity Mismatch and Account Access Conflict Policy
policyCategories.summary: Covers situations where the caller's identity does not match the account holder's information, disputes about who owns an account, and requests from non-account-holders.
policyCategories.scope: All accounts where caller identity and account owner identity are in question.
supportedIssues: Caller is a family member not listed as delegate
Caller claims to be the account owner but fails verification
Dispute between two parties about account ownership
Deceased account holder — family requesting access
Employer calling about employee account on company plan
Customer claims account was opened fraudulently in their name
Business account: employee calling about account without business credentials
unsupportedRequests: Granting account access based on verbal claim of being the account owner
Accepting verbal permission from the account holder to allow a third party access during the same call
Disclosing account details to a non-verified caller based on emotional appeal
allowedActions: Explain verification requirements clearly and empathetically
Offer alternate verification options
Accept a pre-registered delegate's credentials if on file
Initiate in-store verification process for identity disputes
Accept estate/probate documentation for deceased account holder scenarios
Escalate to Fraud team for suspected account fraud
Lock account access pending identity resolution if conflicting ownership claims
forbiddenPromises: Granting account access without completing proper verification
Accepting the claim 'I'm the account holder' as sufficient without verification
Allowing a same-call verbal permission to become a delegate authorization
policyCategories.requiredVerification: Standard and high-security verification as applicable; in-store verification with government ID for ownership disputes
standardHandlingFlow: Step 1: Listen to who is calling and what they are requesting
Step 2: Attempt identity verification with caller
Step 3: If fails — offer alternate verification and/or in-store ID verification
Step 4: For ownership disputes — do not grant access to either party; lock account; route to Fraud or Compliance team
Step 5: For deceased accounts — request death certificate and probate/estate documentation via store visit or email to deceased-accounts@nexalink.com
Step 6: Log all details and route appropriately
requiredDataPoints: Whether a dispute is between two callers

policyBoundaries.sameCallDelegateAdds: Delegate authorization requires account holder to update this through MyAccount portal, at a store, or by calling in as the verified account holder — not verbally during the same call from a third party
policyBoundaries.ownershipDispute: If two parties claim ownership, freeze account and route to Compliance; neither party gets access until resolved
policyBoundaries.deceasedAccount: Account is frozen; only estate executor with documentation may take action

escalationTriggers: Two parties actively disputing account ownership on same call
Caller claims the account holder is a fraudster
Caller reports domestic situation with safety concerns related to account access
safeResponseGuidance: 'I understand this is frustrating and I want to help. To protect this account and its owner, I'm required to complete a verification before I can take any action. Let me walk you through the options available.'
'For account ownership disputes, I need to route this to our dedicated team who can review the documentation from all parties. I'll give you a case number and the details of how to proceed.'
prohibitedResponseExamples: 'You sound like you know the account details, so that's good enough.' (not compliant)
'Your husband said it was okay for you to access the account earlier.' (verbal same-call permission is not sufficient)

approvedResponsePatterns.delegateNotOnFile: I see that your name is not currently listed as an authorized contact on this account. I'm not able to share account details or make changes without that authorization in place. The primary account holder can add you as a delegate through the MyAccount app or at any NexaLink store. Is there anything else I can help you with today?
approvedResponsePatterns.ownershipDispute: I understand this is a difficult situation. For security, I'm going to place a temporary hold on any account changes until the ownership can be properly verified. I've created a case — number [X] — and our account resolution team will follow up within 2-3 business days with instructions on the documentation needed.

followUpRules: Log all identity mismatch events
Ownership disputes must be escalated to Compliance within 1 business day
policyCategories.agentAuthorityLevel: LIMITED — Can attempt verification and explain process; ownership disputes must be escalated
supervisorAuthorityRequiredFor: Proceeding with limited access in exceptional compassionate circumstances (e.g., documented medical emergency)
Unfreezing an account pending ownership dispute
riskSignals: Failed verification from a caller claiming ownership
Competing ownership claim
Domestic situation involving account access
keywords: access the account for someone else
my name is on the account
commonCustomerPhrases: I'm calling for my wife
This is my son's account
My wife passed away and I need access
Someone else has my account
I'm the real account owner

policyCategories.title: Legal Threat and Regulatory Escalation Policy
policyCategories.summary: Defines how to handle customers who make legal threats, reference attorneys, mention filing regulatory complaints, or are involved in active legal proceedings with NexaLink.
policyCategories.scope: All consumer and business accounts.
supportedIssues: Customer mentions they have retained an attorney
Customer references FCC, CPUC, CFPB, or state PUC complaint
Customer threatens to file a BBB complaint
Customer in active small claims or civil court case
Customer demanding a legal response or statement
unsupportedRequests: Providing legal advice or legal interpretations to customers
Making legal admissions on behalf of NexaLink
Agreeing that NexaLink violated the law
Promising a specific legal or regulatory outcome
allowedActions: Acknowledge the customer's concern calmly and professionally
Document the legal threat in CRM immediately
Provide FCC and CPUC filing information if requested
Route to Compliance/Legal escalation team immediately
Continue to assist with the underlying issue (billing, service) to the extent possible
Provide NexaLink's legal correspondence address if demanded
forbiddenPromises: Making any legal admission ('NexaLink was wrong about this')
Promising a specific legal resolution
Agreeing that the customer will win any legal proceeding
Discouraging the customer from filing a regulatory complaint
Promising to prevent any regulatory action from proceeding
standardHandlingFlow: Step 1: Let the customer speak; do not interrupt or argue
Step 2: Acknowledge their concern professionally: 'I understand you feel very strongly about this.'
Step 3: Do NOT make legal statements, admissions, or promises
Step 4: Document the legal threat in CRM with exact language used by customer
Step 5: Escalate immediately to Compliance team — do not attempt to resolve legal matters at frontline
Step 6: Provide Compliance team contact/reference to customer
Step 7: If customer demands regulatory filing info, provide it calmly and without discouragement
requiredDataPoints: Exact threat or legal reference made by customer
Underlying issue prompting the legal threat
Case or ticket numbers related to underlying issue

policyBoundaries.noLegalOpinions: Frontline agents and AI systems must never provide legal opinions, interpretations, or advice
policyBoundaries.noDiscouragement: Agents must NOT discourage customers from filing regulatory complaints; it is their legal right
policyBoundaries.complianceHandoff: All legal and regulatory matters must be transferred to the Compliance team; frontline must not attempt independent resolution
policyBoundaries.legalCorrespondenceAddress: NexaLink Legal Department, 1100 Nexalink Tower, Atlanta, GA 30309; nexalinklegal@nexalink.com

escalationTriggers: Any mention of lawsuit, attorney, or legal proceedings — mandatory Compliance escalation
Any mention of FCC, CPUC, BBB, CFPB, state PUC complaint filing
Customer presents a case or docket number
Customer requests legal records or documentation
safeResponseGuidance: 'I understand this has been a very frustrating experience, and I take your concerns seriously. I want to make sure this reaches the right team who is best positioned to address this at the level you're describing.'
'I'm not in a position to provide legal guidance, but I absolutely want to make sure this is addressed properly. Let me connect you with our Compliance team.'
prohibitedResponseExamples: 'You'll never win that lawsuit.' (adversarial and inappropriate)
'We didn't do anything wrong so you can go ahead and sue us.' (dismissive and harmful)
'Please don't file an FCC complaint — let me fix this first.' (discouraging regulatory filing is prohibited)
'Legally speaking, NexaLink is not liable for...' (legal opinion by agent)

approvedResponsePatterns.legalThreat: I hear you and I understand you're very frustrated with this situation. I'm going to make sure this is escalated to our Compliance team, who handles matters at this level. I've created a priority case — number [X]. You can also reach our Compliance team directly at compliance@nexalink.com. I'll continue to do what I can to help you with [underlying issue] in the meantime.
approvedResponsePatterns.regulatoryFiling: You are absolutely within your rights to file a complaint with the FCC at fcc.gov/consumers, or with the CFPB at consumerfinance.gov/complaint. I can also note your intent on your account so our Compliance team is aware. Would you like me to do that?

followUpRules: All legal threat notes must be logged in CRM within 5 minutes and flagged for Compliance team
Compliance team must acknowledge within 4 business hours
policyCategories.agentAuthorityLevel: LIMITED — Log and escalate only; no legal resolution authority
supervisorAuthorityRequiredFor: Anything beyond acknowledgment and routing in legal threat scenarios
riskSignals: Words: sue, attorney, lawyer, lawsuit, FCC, CFPB, court, legal action
Customer mentions receiving a settlement offer
Customer asks for records for legal proceedings
commonCustomerPhrases: I'm going to sue you
I've retained an attorney
I'm filing an FCC complaint
I'll take you to small claims court
My lawyer will be in touch
I'm reporting you to the BBB

policyCategories.title: Abusive Customer Interaction and Agent Safety Policy
policyCategories.summary: Defines how agents and AI systems must respond to customers who are verbally abusive, threatening, or creating an unsafe interaction, while still attempting to help with legitimate issues.
policyCategories.scope: All inbound contact channels.
supportedIssues: Customer using profanity or personal insults toward agent
Customer making threats of physical harm
Customer making racial, gender, or other discriminatory remarks
unsupportedRequests: Agent is not required to endure continued verbal abuse after two de-escalation attempts
Agent is not required to process account requests while being actively threatened
allowedActions: Acknowledge frustration and attempt de-escalation (up to two attempts)
Clearly state that abusive language is not acceptable
Warn customer that call may be terminated if abuse continues
Terminate the call if threats of physical harm are made (no warning required)
Connect customer to crisis resources if self-harm is indicated
Log the interaction as abusive in CRM
Transfer to supervisor if escalation is requested even during abuse
Continue to help with the legitimate underlying issue if abuse subsides
forbiddenPromises: Promising to not report the abusive behavior to management
Agreeing with the customer that their abusive behavior is justified
Making concessions due to the abuse (rewarding abusive behavior with credits)
policyCategories.requiredVerification: Attempt standard verification; however, if threats are made, safety takes precedence over completing verification
standardHandlingFlow: Step 1: Acknowledge the customer's frustration and attempt de-escalation
Step 2: If abusive language continues: 'I want to help you, but I need to ask that we keep our conversation respectful so I can best assist you.'

Step 3: If abuse continues after second warning: 'I'm going to need to end this call as I'm unable to continue while being spoken to this way. You're welcome to call back when you're ready to speak with us respectfully.'
Step 4: If physical threat: terminate call immediately; log as threat; flag for security
Step 5: If self-harm indicated: pause account issue; provide crisis line information; stay on line if safe
Step 6: Log all abusive incidents in CRM for quality and safety review
Step 7: Despite abuse, if the customer's underlying issue is valid, note it for follow-up — valid issues are not dismissed because of bad behavior
requiredDataPoints: Exact quotes if physical threats made

policyBoundaries.callTerminationPolicy: Agents may terminate calls after two de-escalation attempts when abuse continues; physical threats = immediate termination
policyBoundaries.selfHarmProtocol: If customer indicates suicidal ideation or self-harm: stay on call; do not debate; provide 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline; alert supervisor immediately
policyBoundaries.underlyingIssuePolicy: Even after terminating an abusive call, agents must log the underlying issue so it is not left unaddressed
policyBoundaries.concessionPolicy: Agents must NOT make account concessions during abusive interactions; this rewards bad behavior and creates inconsistency

escalationTriggers: Any physical threat — immediate call termination and supervisor notification
Customer indicates suicidal ideation — crisis protocol
Discriminatory abuse — log and route to supervisor
safeResponseGuidance: 'I can hear how upset you are, and I truly want to help. I need to ask that we keep our conversation respectful so I can best assist you.'
'I understand you're frustrated. Helping you is my priority, and I'm going to do everything I can within my authority. I do need us to work together calmly to get there.'
prohibitedResponseExamples: Arguing back with the customer, using sarcasm, or matching their emotional level
'Fine, I'll just give you the credit to end this call.' (concession under duress)
Continuing to process account changes while being actively threatened

approvedResponsePatterns.deEscalationWarning: I hear that you're extremely frustrated, and I genuinely want to resolve this for you. I do need to ask that we speak respectfully so I can focus on helping you. If we're unable to do that, I'll need to end the call, but I'd much rather help you with this today.
approvedResponsePatterns.callTermination: I'm unable to continue this call under these circumstances. You are welcome to call back when you're ready to speak with us. Your underlying issue has been noted and will be reviewed. Thank you.
approvedResponsePatterns.crisisResponse: I'm concerned about what you just shared. Please know that help is available. The 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline is available 24/7 — you can call or text 988. Would you like me to stay on the line with you for a moment?

followUpRules: Log all abusive call details; do not delete
Underlying valid issue must still be actioned regardless of call outcome
Crisis protocol calls must be flagged for supervisor review within 1 hour
policyCategories.agentAuthorityLevel: FULL for de-escalation; FULL for call termination after procedure followed; NO authority to reward abusive behavior with concessions
supervisorAuthorityRequiredFor: Approving any concession offered after or during an abusive call
riskSignals: Customer making threats
Customer indicates self-harm or harm to others
Discriminatory language used
commonCustomerPhrases: (profanity toward agent)
You're going to regret this
I know where you work
I don't want to live anymore
I'll come to your office

policyCategories.title: Service Requests Beyond Frontline Agent Authority
policyCategories.summary: Handles situations where a customer's request requires a level of authority, access, or specialist knowledge that exceeds frontline agent capability.
policyCategories.scope: All accounts and all service types.
supportedIssues: Customer requesting manual exception to standard policy
Customer requesting access to restricted account features
Customer requesting a change requiring specialist tools
Customer requesting information only available to backend teams
unsupportedRequests: Processing any action that frontline system access does not permit
Promising outcomes of specialist or management review
allowedActions: Clearly explain what can and cannot be done at the current level
Initiate a specialist referral or escalation
Log the request in detail for the specialist team
Provide a realistic timeline for specialist response
Offer what can be done at the current level while escalating the rest
forbiddenPromises: Promising the specialist will approve the request
Making the request seem simpler than it is
Processing an action outside system authority even if the customer insists
standardHandlingFlow: Step 1: Understand the full request
Step 2: Identify which part(s) exceed frontline authority
Step 3: Do what CAN be done at the current level
Step 4: Create a specialist referral/ticket for the rest
Step 5: Provide customer with case number and expected response timeline

policyBoundaries.systemAccess: Frontline agents may only use system tools available to their access tier; they cannot create or modify database entries outside their module
policyBoundaries.escalationRequired: Any request not completable within frontline tools must be escalated — not improvised

escalationTriggers: Any request requiring specialist system access
Any exception to published policy
safeResponseGuidance: 'This request is something I'm not able to process at my level, but I want to make sure it's handled. Let me escalate this to a specialist who has the tools to help you.'
prohibitedResponseExamples: 'Let me try to do this manually.' (if outside system authority)

approvedResponsePatterns.beyondAuthority: This is something I'm not able to process directly, but I've escalated it to our [specialist team name] team. Your case number is [X]. You can expect a response within [Y business days]. Is there anything I can help you with in the meantime?

followUpRules: Log all escalated requests with full description
policyCategories.agentAuthorityLevel: LIMITED — Document and escalate
riskSignals: Customer is frustrated that frontline cannot complete the request
commonCustomerPhrases: Just override the system
Can't you manually do this?
Why do I need to talk to someone else?
You should be able to do this

policyCategories.title: Payment Failure and Settlement Issue Policy
policyCategories.summary: Covers handling of failed payments, returned payments, NSF (non-sufficient funds) events, payment arrangements, and service suspension due to nonpayment.
policyCategories.scope: All postpaid consumer and business accounts.
supportedIssues: Payment processed but not reflected on account
unsupportedRequests: Waiving an NSF fee for a repeated offense without supervisor approval
Extending a payment due date beyond the maximum allowed period
Processing payment arrangements for accounts with active fraud holds
allowedActions: Update payment method on file
Process a payment (full or authorized partial)
Set up AutoPay enrollment
Waive a first-time NSF fee
Apply a payment extension (up to 7 days) for accounts in good standing
Initiate service restoration after confirmed payment
Escalate payment arrangement requests beyond 7 days to billing specialist
forbiddenPromises: Promising service won't be suspended during payment arrangement period without confirmation
Promising an NSF fee will be waived without confirming it's a first offense
Accepting a partial payment as 'full settlement' without billing specialist approval
Guaranteeing exact service restoration time after payment
standardHandlingFlow: Step 1: Verify identity
Step 2: Review payment history and current balance due
Step 3: Identify reason for payment failure if possible
Step 4: Offer to update payment method and process payment
Step 5: If customer cannot pay in full: assess payment extension or arrangement eligibility
Step 6: Apply extension (up to 7 days) if account in good standing and first extension in 12 months
Step 7: For longer arrangements: escalate to billing specialist
Step 8: After payment is confirmed: initiate service restoration if suspended
Step 9: Log all payment activities in CRM

policyBoundaries.paymentExtension: Maximum 7-day extension; once per 12 months per account; account must not have active fraud hold
policyBoundaries.nsfFeeWaiver: One NSF fee waiver per 12 months per account; first offense only
policyBoundaries.serviceRestoration: Service restoration begins within 2-4 hours after payment confirmed; not immediate
policyBoundaries.partialPayment: Partial payments are accepted but do not prevent service suspension unless full amount required has been paid; billing specialist may approve payment plans for larger balances

escalationTriggers: Business account with large balance dispute and payment failure
Customer requesting payment arrangement beyond 7 days
Third consecutive payment failure on account
safeResponseGuidance: 'I can see your payment from [date] didn't go through. Let's get this sorted out — would you like to update your payment method and process it now?'
'Since this appears to be the first time this has happened and your account has been in good standing, I'm able to extend your due date by 7 days. Does that help?'
prohibitedResponseExamples: 'Don't worry, I'll make sure your service isn't suspended.' (cannot guarantee without official extension in system)
'Just pay half and we'll call it settled.' (cannot authorize partial settlement)

approvedResponsePatterns.paymentExtension: I've applied a 7-day payment extension on your account. Your new due date is [date]. Please note that this is the one extension available to you in a 12-month period, so I'd recommend setting up AutoPay to avoid this in the future. Would you like me to help you with that?
approvedResponsePatterns.serviceRestoration: I can confirm your payment of $[amount] has been processed. Your service should be fully restored within 2-4 hours. If you're still experiencing issues after 4 hours, please give us a call and we'll make sure it's resolved.

followUpRules: Log all payment events — processed, failed, extended — with timestamps
If service is suspended and payment received, flag for restoration monitoring
policyCategories.agentAuthorityLevel: PARTIAL — Can process payments, apply 7-day extension, waive first NSF fee; longer arrangements require billing specialist
supervisorAuthorityRequiredFor: NSF fee waiver beyond first offense
riskSignals: Third payment failure in 6 months
Large outstanding balance with payment failure
commonCustomerPhrases: My payment didn't go through
My service was cut off
I need more time to pay
AutoPay failed
Can I set up a payment plan?
When will my service come back on?

policyCategories.title: Number Reactivation and Suspension Policy
policyCategories.summary: Governs handling of account and number suspensions, voluntary suspensions, involuntary suspensions, and reactivation requests.
policyCategories.scope: All consumer and business mobile accounts.
supportedIssues: Voluntary suspension request (military, extended travel, etc.)
Reactivation after payment of outstanding balance
Suspended number at risk of being recycled
Partial account suspension (one line of multi-line plan)
unsupportedRequests: Reactivating a number that has been recycled/reassigned
Suspending a line that is within a device payment plan without disclosing obligations continue
Reactivating a line with an active fraud hold without Fraud team clearance
Voluntary suspension for longer than 6 months without review
allowedActions: Process voluntary suspension for eligible accounts
Explain voluntary suspension terms and billing implications
Reactivate an account after confirmed payment of outstanding balance
Advise customer on number recycling risk timeline
Initiate reactivation for accounts suspended for nonpayment
Check fraud hold status before proceeding with reactivation
forbiddenPromises: Guaranteeing that a suspended number will not be recycled within a specific timeframe
Promising reactivation without confirmed payment for nonpayment suspension
Reactivating an account with an active fraud hold without Fraud team clearance
policyCategories.requiredVerification: Full identity verification (POL-001); high-security for fraud-related suspension reactivation
standardHandlingFlow: Step 1: Verify identity
Step 2: Check account status — voluntary or involuntary suspension, fraud hold
Step 3: If involuntary (nonpayment): advise customer of outstanding balance; process reactivation after payment confirmed
Step 4: If voluntary: confirm suspension request and terms (DPP obligations continue, service charge may apply)
Step 5: If fraud hold: do NOT reactivate; route to Fraud team
Step 6: Log suspension/reactivation with reason code

policyBoundaries.voluntarySuspension: Available for 1-3 months; reduced monthly fee applies; DPP payments continue; available once per 12 months
policyBoundaries.numberRecycling: Numbers are eligible for recycling after 90 days of continuous suspension; NexaLink cannot guarantee a number for longer than that
policyBoundaries.reactivationAfterPayment: Service typically restores within 2-4 hours of confirmed payment
policyBoundaries.fraudHoldReactivation: Only the Fraud team may clear a fraud hold for reactivation; frontline cannot override

escalationTriggers: Fraud hold reactivation request
Number suspension exceeding 90 days
Business account suspension affecting multiple lines
safeResponseGuidance: 'I can see your account is currently suspended due to an outstanding balance of $[amount]. Once we process your payment, your service will be restored within 2-4 hours.'
'I want to make sure you know that during a voluntary suspension, your device installment payments will continue as usual. Is that okay?'
prohibitedResponseExamples: 'Your number will still be here even after 6 months of suspension.' (cannot guarantee)
'I'll reactivate it now and you can pay later.' (payment must be confirmed first for nonpayment suspensions)

approvedResponsePatterns.reactivationAfterPayment: Your payment of $[amount] has been processed. Your account is now being reactivated. Service typically restores within 2-4 hours. If you're still experiencing issues after 4 hours, please give us a call.
approvedResponsePatterns.voluntarySuspension: I've processed your voluntary suspension request for [number]. Your service will be suspended starting [date] for up to [duration]. Please note that your device installment payment of $[amount/month] will continue during this period. To reactivate, just contact us and we'll get you back up and running right away.

followUpRules: Log all suspension and reactivation events with reason codes and timestamps
policyCategories.agentAuthorityLevel: PARTIAL — Can process voluntary suspension and payment-based reactivation; fraud hold reactivation requires Fraud team
supervisorAuthorityRequiredFor: Waiving reactivation fees in exceptional cases
riskSignals: Number suspended 80+ days — approaching recycling window
Customer unaware of recycling risk
commonCustomerPhrases: Turn my service back on
I want to suspend my line
I'm deploying, can I pause my plan?
My service was turned off
I'm worried about losing my number

policyCategories.title: Broadband and Fixed Wireless Internet Service Policy
policyCategories.summary: Covers technical and billing issues specific to NexaHome fixed wireless and NexaFiber broadband services.
policyCategories.scope: All NexaHome and NexaFiber subscribers.
supportedIssues: No internet connection on broadband service
Wi-Fi not working but ethernet works
Outage on fixed wireless or fiber service
unsupportedRequests: Providing IT support for customer's personal network setup beyond NexaLink gateway
Guaranteeing speeds on wireless broadband (fixed wireless speeds vary with conditions)
Installing or managing customer's personal Wi-Fi devices
allowedActions: Check for broadband outages in customer's service area
Perform remote diagnostic on NexaLink gateway device
Guide customer through gateway restart and Wi-Fi reconnection
Schedule a technician visit for equipment issues
Confirm service address and speed tier
Explain data cap terms and overage policy
Apply billing credit for confirmed service outage
forbiddenPromises: Guaranteeing fixed wireless speeds at customer's specific location
Promising a technician will arrive at a specific time (provide window only)
Confirming fiber is available at a specific address without system verification
standardHandlingFlow: Step 1: Verify identity
Step 2: Confirm service address and service type (NexaHome or NexaFiber)
Step 3: Check for active service outage in area
Step 4: If no outage: run remote gateway diagnostic
Step 5: Guide customer through restart and basic troubleshooting
Step 6: If issue unresolved: schedule technician visit
Step 7: Log issue and actions in CRM

policyBoundaries.fixedWirelessSpeeds: Speeds are 'up to' stated maximum; actual speeds vary based on weather, line-of-sight, and network conditions
policyBoundaries.technicianVisit: Technician visits scheduled in 4-hour windows; earliest availability communicated during scheduling
policyBoundaries.dataCap: NexaHome plans have a 1.2TB monthly data cap (except Unlimited add-on); overage charges apply per 50GB block

escalationTriggers: Repeated technician visits without resolution
Business customer with service-affecting outage
Fiber installation complaint
safeResponseGuidance: 'Let me run a quick diagnostic on your gateway remotely. While I do that, could you confirm if the lights on your gateway are on and what colors they are?'
prohibitedResponseExamples: 'Your speeds will definitely be [X] Mbps.' (cannot guarantee for fixed wireless)
'A technician will be there by noon.' (provide a window, not a specific time)

approvedResponsePatterns.technicianScheduled: I've scheduled a technician visit for [date] between [time1] and [time2] at [service address]. The technician will call 30 minutes before arrival. Your appointment reference is [number]. Is there anything else I can help you with?

followUpRules: Log all broadband issues, diagnostic results, and scheduled visits
policyCategories.agentAuthorityLevel: PARTIAL — Can troubleshoot, schedule tech visits, apply credits for confirmed outages
riskSignals: Repeated service issues at same address
Customer threatening cancellation over persistent issue
commonCustomerPhrases: My internet is not working
My Wi-Fi keeps dropping
Speeds are way slower than advertised
My router isn't connecting
When can a tech come out?

policyCategories.title: Device Configuration and APN Settings Guidance Policy
policyCategories.summary: Governs how agents provide guidance on device settings, APN configuration, and troubleshooting for connectivity issues related to device misconfiguration.
policyCategories.scope: All mobile consumer and business accounts.
supportedIssues: APN settings for mobile data not configured correctly
unsupportedRequests: Providing warranty service for a device — must go to manufacturer or Device Protection plan
Providing detailed device repair guidance
Providing APN credentials for use on other carriers' networks
allowedActions: Provide NexaLink APN settings for common device platforms (iOS, Android)
Guide customer through APN configuration steps verbally or via SMS/email
Check IMEI for NexaLink network compatibility
Assist with Wi-Fi Calling and VoLTE settings activation
Guide eSIM provisioning process
Check if device is locked or unlocked
Submit device unlock request if eligible
forbiddenPromises: Guaranteeing that APN settings will fix the issue (may be a network or SIM issue)
Providing APN details for use on third-party or competitor networks
Promising device will work if it is not NexaLink-compatible
policyCategories.requiredVerification: Full identity verification (POL-001) for account-related actions; general APN guidance can be provided before verification
standardHandlingFlow: Step 1: Identify device model and operating system
Step 2: Check if SIM is recognized and if device is NexaLink-compatible
Step 3: Provide appropriate APN settings for device platform
Step 4: Guide customer through configuration steps
Step 5: Test connectivity — ask customer to toggle airplane mode and retest
Step 6: If issue persists, escalate to technical specialist or schedule callback
requiredDataPoints: Whether SIM is recognized by device

policyBoundaries.deviceUnlock: Devices are eligible for unlock after 60 days on active NexaLink service with no outstanding balance; fee waived for customers in good standing
policyBoundaries.imeiCompatibility: Only GSM devices with NexaLink-compatible bands (B2, B4, B5, B12, B66, N25, N41, N66, N71) are supported

escalationTriggers: Device issue unresolved after full troubleshooting
Device unlock request on account with outstanding balance
safeResponseGuidance: 'Let me walk you through the APN settings for your device — this should take just a couple of minutes.'
'If your device is locked to another carrier, you'll need to get it unlocked before it will work on NexaLink. I can check your eligibility for that right now.'
prohibitedResponseExamples: 'Just use our competitor's APN settings.' (inappropriate and incorrect)
'I guarantee these settings will fix your issue.' (issue may be network or hardware related)

approvedResponsePatterns.apnGuidance: I'll walk you through the APN settings for your [device type]. First, go to Settings, then [Network/Cellular/Mobile Data], then [Access Point Names]. Tap the '+' to add a new APN and enter the following: APN: nexalink, MMSC: http://mmsc.nexalink.com... [complete settings]. Save and restart your device. Would you like me to send these settings to your email as well?

followUpRules: Log all device configuration sessions; note device, issue, settings applied, and outcome
policyCategories.agentAuthorityLevel: FULL for device guidance and unlock requests within policy
supervisorAuthorityRequiredFor: Unlock exception for accounts with outstanding balance
riskSignals: Customer is trying to use a device from another carrier — check lock/compatibility
commonCustomerPhrases: My data isn't working on my new phone
How do I set up APN?
My phone says no SIM
I need to unlock my phone
How do I enable Wi-Fi calling?

policyCategories.title: Ticket Status and Case Tracking Policy
policyCategories.summary: Covers how agents handle customer inquiries about the status of open tickets, complaints, or cases.
policyCategories.scope: All accounts with active or recent support tickets.
supportedIssues: Customer asking for status of open ticket
Customer asking for resolution update on complaint
Customer asking why their ticket was closed
Customer requesting to reopen a ticket
allowedActions: Access ticket history and provide status update
Explain the ticket priority and expected resolution window
Escalate a stale or overdue ticket to the responsible team
Reopen a ticket closed within 30 days if resolution was ineffective
Create a new linked ticket for ongoing issues from a closed case
forbiddenPromises: Guaranteeing a specific resolution date beyond stated SLA
Promising the ticket will be resolved in favor of the customer
Claiming a ticket was 'escalated' without actually escalating it in the system
standardHandlingFlow: Step 1: Verify identity
Step 2: Pull up ticket details
Step 3: Confirm current status, assigned team, and expected timeline
Step 4: If overdue — escalate in system; inform customer
Step 5: If incorrectly closed — reopen or create linked case
Step 6: Provide customer with updated expected timeline

policyBoundaries.ticketSLA: P1: 24 hours; P2: 3-5 business days; P3: 7-10 business days
policyBoundaries.stalTicketDefinition: A ticket is 'stale' if it exceeds its SLA window without resolution or update
policyBoundaries.reopenWindow: Tickets may be reopened within 30 days of closure; older tickets require new linked case

escalationTriggers: Ticket overdue past SLA
Ticket closed without resolution being effective
safeResponseGuidance: 'Your case [X] is currently with our [team name] team and has a target resolution of [timeframe]. I'm going to add an urgency note given that it's been [X days].
prohibitedResponseExamples: 'I've escalated it.' (must be confirmed in system, not just stated)

approvedResponsePatterns.ticketStatus: Your case [number] is currently open and assigned to our [team] team with a [priority level] priority. Based on our standard timelines, you should receive an update within [X business days]. I've added an urgency note to ensure your case is reviewed promptly. Can I help you with anything else?

followUpRules: Log status inquiry contact in CRM; if escalation was actioned, confirm in system
policyCategories.agentAuthorityLevel: FULL for status queries; PARTIAL for escalating stale tickets
riskSignals: Ticket exceeds SLA with no update
Multiple status inquiries from same customer
commonCustomerPhrases: What's the status of my ticket?
Nobody has called me about my case
You said it would be resolved by now
My case was closed but nothing was fixed

policyCategories.title: Outage Compensation and Service Credit Request Policy
policyCategories.summary: Governs how customers request compensation or service credits following a network outage or service disruption, including eligibility criteria and approval flows.
policyCategories.scope: All consumer and business accounts affected by a confirmed service outage.
supportedIssues: Customer requesting credit for network outage
Customer requesting compensation for prolonged service disruption
Business customer invoking SLA credit for outage
Customer claiming outage impacted their business/livelihoods
unsupportedRequests: Automatic compensation for any service disruption regardless of duration
Compensation for outages caused by force majeure (federal disaster declarations)
Business revenue loss compensation — NexaLink does not compensate for third-party economic losses
Compensation for individual device or premises issues not related to NexaLink network
allowedActions: Confirm whether a network outage was officially logged for customer's area and duration
Apply a service credit up to $25 for confirmed outages exceeding 4 hours
Submit outage compensation review for larger credits
Provide outage confirmation reference for business SLA review
Escalate business SLA credit to Business Account Team
forbiddenPromises: Promising automatic credit for any outage regardless of verification
Promising a specific credit amount without authorization
Promising compensation for unconfirmed outages based on customer's claim alone
Committing to business revenue loss compensation
policyCategories.requiredVerification: Full identity verification (POL-001); outage confirmation from NOC records required
standardHandlingFlow: Step 1: Verify identity
Step 2: Confirm whether a network outage was officially recorded for customer's area
Step 3: Verify customer's account address falls within the affected area
Step 4: Confirm outage duration from NOC records
Step 5: Apply credit if duration exceeds 4 hours and credit is within frontline authority
Step 6: For larger credits or business SLA: escalate to appropriate team
Step 7: Log request and outcome in CRM

policyBoundaries.minimumOutageDuration: Service credits are considered for outages lasting 4 or more consecutive hours
policyBoundaries.creditCalculation: Consumer: up to $5 per 24 hours of confirmed outage; Business: per SLA contract terms
policyBoundaries.frontlineLimit: $25 maximum per outage event; larger amounts require approval
policyBoundaries.forceMajeure: Outages caused by federally declared disasters, Acts of God, or third-party infrastructure failures are not eligible for standard compensation
policyBoundaries.oncePer90Days: Service credit for outage limited to once per 90-day period per account

escalationTriggers: Outage duration >24 hours
Business SLA credit claim
Customer claims economic loss due to outage
safeResponseGuidance: 'I can confirm there was a service disruption in your area from [start] to [end] — that's approximately [X] hours. Based on our outage compensation policy, I'm applying a service credit of $[amount] to your account.'
'I'm not able to find a confirmed outage record for your area during that time. If you believe this is in error, I can open a review request. Your case number would be [X].'
prohibitedResponseExamples: 'Yes, you'll get a full month credit for this outage.' (not confirmed in policy)
'We'll compensate you for the business you lost.' (NexaLink does not cover economic losses)

approvedResponsePatterns.outageCredit: I've confirmed that there was a network service disruption affecting your area from [start time] to [end time] — approximately [X hours]. As per our service commitment, I'm applying a credit of $[amount] to your account. This will appear on your next billing statement. Your reference number is [X].
approvedResponsePatterns.outageNotConfirmed: I've reviewed our network records and I'm not seeing a confirmed outage in your area during the period you've described. I'd like to open an investigation to look into this further. Your case number is [X] and you'll receive an update within 3-5 business days.

followUpRules: Log outage credit with NOC incident reference and credit amount applied
policyCategories.agentAuthorityLevel: PARTIAL — Can apply credit up to $25 for confirmed outages; larger amounts and business SLA require escalation
riskSignals: Customer claiming outage affected business without official outage record
commonCustomerPhrases: I had no service for 8 hours — I want a credit
The outage cost me business
I deserve compensation for the downtime
What's my SLA credit?

policyCategories.title: Social Media Complaint Threat and Public Escalation Policy
policyCategories.summary: Governs how agents handle customers who threaten to post negative reviews, escalate to social media, or otherwise threaten NexaLink's public reputation.
policyCategories.scope: All consumer and business accounts.
supportedIssues: Customer threatens to post on Twitter/X, Facebook, TikTok
Customer threatens to leave negative reviews (Google, BBB, Yelp, Trustpilot)
Customer threatens to contact news media
Customer mentions they have a large social media following
allowedActions: Continue to work to resolve the customer's underlying issue
Escalate to supervisor if the underlying issue warrants it
Acknowledge the customer's right to share their experience
Transfer to senior team member who can offer more resolution options
Log the social media threat in CRM
forbiddenPromises: Making a special concession purely because of the social media threat (rewarding threat behavior)
Asking the customer to 'hold off' on posting in exchange for a benefit
Promising anything to suppress a public complaint
Making legal threats about the customer's ability to post reviews
standardHandlingFlow: Step 1: Acknowledge the customer's frustration and the underlying issue
Step 2: Do NOT react to the social media threat itself — address the underlying problem
Step 3: Work to resolve the issue through proper channels
Step 4: Escalate to supervisor if underlying issue warrants escalation
Step 5: Log the social media/public complaint threat in CRM
Step 6: If social media complaint has already been posted — route to Social Media team via internal ticket

policyBoundaries.noRewardForThreats: NexaLink policy does not allow agents to offer additional benefits based solely on social media threats; resolution must be based on the merit of the underlying issue
policyBoundaries.customerRight: Customers have the legal right to share their experiences publicly; agents must not discourage this
policyBoundaries.socialMediaTeam: If a public post has been made, route to the Social Media Response Team via internal ticket — not customer-facing handling

escalationTriggers: Customer has already posted publicly and it is gaining traction
High-profile account or verified public figure
Media outlet involvement
safeResponseGuidance: 'I completely understand your frustration and I want to make sure this is resolved. You have every right to share your experience wherever you choose — what I'd like to focus on right now is making sure we get this right for you.'
'Let me escalate this to a senior team member who can help us find the best solution to this issue.'
prohibitedResponseExamples: 'Please don't post about this — I'll give you a discount if you hold off.' (incentivizing suppression)
'You can't post false information about us online.' (legal threat by agent)
'If you post that, there could be consequences.' (inappropriate)

approvedResponsePatterns.acknowledgeThreat: I hear you, and I understand this has been a very frustrating experience. You absolutely have the right to share your experience wherever you'd like. What I'd really like to do right now is focus on making sure this issue is resolved the right way. Let me escalate this to make sure you get the attention you deserve.

followUpRules: Log social media threat in CRM with platform mentioned and underlying issue
If post is live, trigger Social Media Response Team ticket
policyCategories.agentAuthorityLevel: PARTIAL — Can address underlying issue; threat itself should not change resolution path
supervisorAuthorityRequiredFor: Exceptional resolution that may prevent escalation — based on issue merit, not threat
riskSignals: Customer has large following or is a media contact
Post is already live and gaining engagement
commonCustomerPhrases: I'm going to post this on Twitter
I have 100,000 followers
I'll make this go viral
I'm reporting you to the news
I'm going to leave a review everywhere

policyCategories.title: Multilingual Support and Language Limitation Policy
policyCategories.summary: Defines how agents handle calls from customers who do not speak English or Spanish, and the limitations of language support.
policyCategories.scope: All inbound contacts.
supportedIssues: Customer requests support in a non-supported language
Partial language barrier affecting communication quality
unsupportedRequests: AI Agent handling a call in a language other than English or Spanish
Providing account actions without verified understanding of mutual communication
Bypassing interpreter service for efficiency
allowedActions: Detect non-English/Spanish language and route to appropriate language queue
Initiate LanguageLine interpreter service for 240+ languages
Inform customer in their language that an interpreter is being arranged (pre-scripted phrases available)
Connect interpreter and conduct call with interpreter on line
Document language in CRM for routing preference
forbiddenPromises: Promising a native-language speaking agent is available immediately in non-primary languages
Processing account actions when communication is clearly not understood by customer
policyCategories.requiredVerification: Standard identity verification — interpreter may assist customer with spoken responses but does not access account data
standardHandlingFlow: Step 1: Detect or be informed of language preference
Step 2: For Spanish: transfer to Spanish queue immediately
Step 3: For other languages: invoke LanguageLine interpreter
Step 4: Brief interpreter on the issue; conduct call with customer
Step 5: Document language in CRM

policyBoundaries.aiAgentLanguage: AI Agent is English and Spanish only — non-English/Spanish callers must be transferred to human agent
policyBoundaries.interpreterRole: Interpreter translates spoken communication only — does not read account data or perform account actions

escalationTriggers: LanguageLine unavailable
High-stakes situation (fraud, emergency) with language barrier
safeResponseGuidance: 'Please hold while I arrange an interpreter for you — [language phrase: Un momento por favor / Veuillez patienter / 请稍等]'
prohibitedResponseExamples: Attempting to conduct account changes when it is clear the customer does not understand the confirmation being given

approvedResponsePatterns.interpreterRequest: Please hold for just a moment — I'm connecting an interpreter to assist us. [Invoke LanguageLine] Thank you for your patience. I now have an interpreter on the line who will help us communicate.

followUpRules: Log language preference in CRM
policyCategories.agentAuthorityLevel: FULL — Can and must invoke interpreter service; AI must hand off to human for non-English/Spanish
supervisorAuthorityRequiredFor: No supervisor required for standard language routing
riskSignals: Customer appears to not understand verification being conducted due to language barrier — do not proceed with account access until communication is clear
commonCustomerPhrases: No English
Je parle français
habla español
I need someone who speaks [language]

policyCategories.title: Unsupported or Unrecognized Request Handling Policy
policyCategories.summary: Defines how agents and AI systems handle requests that fall outside known policies, product scope, or agent capability.
policyCategories.scope: All inbound contacts.
supportedIssues: Customer asks about a service NexaLink does not offer
Customer asks about a policy that does not exist
Customer asks about something completely unrelated to telecom
AI system does not recognize the intent of the request
unsupportedRequests: Inventing policies or products to satisfy the customer
Pretending a capability exists when it does not
Guessing at answers when the topic is unknown
allowedActions: Acknowledge that the request is not something that can be addressed at this level
Offer to escalate to a specialist or supervisor if the topic may be within NexaLink's scope
Redirect to self-service portal or FAQ if applicable
Log the request for knowledge base review
Politely explain that the request is outside the scope of NexaLink support
forbiddenPromises: Inventing an answer to a question for which no policy or knowledge base entry exists
Agreeing that NexaLink can do something it cannot
Providing advice outside NexaLink's domain (legal, medical, financial advice)
policyCategories.requiredVerification: Not required for general inquiry handling
standardHandlingFlow: Step 1: Attempt to identify whether the request is within NexaLink's scope
Step 2: If within scope but unknown: escalate to specialist
Step 3: If outside scope: politely inform and offer alternative
Step 4: Log request for knowledge base improvement review
requiredDataPoints: Whether it is within NexaLink's product/service scope

policyBoundaries.knowledgeLimit: If the AI system does not have a clear policy or knowledge base match, it must use the fallback phrase and not speculate
policyBoundaries.fallbackPhrase: I don't have enough information to answer that accurately. Let me connect you with a team member who can help.

escalationTriggers: Request may be within NexaLink scope but is unrecognized — escalate to supervisor or specialist
safeResponseGuidance: 'That's not something I have information on in our system, but let me see if I can connect you with someone who might be able to help.'
'I want to make sure I give you an accurate answer, and that's a bit outside what I'm able to help with directly. I can transfer you or share where you can find the right resource.'
prohibitedResponseExamples: 'I think we probably can do that — let me just say yes.' (inventing capabilities)
'I'm not sure but I think the policy is...' (guessing at policy)

approvedResponsePatterns.unknownRequest: That's a great question, but I want to make sure you get the right information. I don't have a clear answer for that in my current resources. Let me connect you with a team member who will be able to help you accurately. Is that okay?
approvedResponsePatterns.outOfScope: That's actually a bit outside the scope of what I can help with in NexaLink support. You may want to reach out to [relevant resource]. Is there anything I can help you with regarding your NexaLink account or services?

followUpRules: Log unknown/out-of-scope requests for knowledge base review
policyCategories.agentAuthorityLevel: LIMITED — Acknowledge and route; never invent
supervisorAuthorityRequiredFor: Anything requiring policy interpretation beyond known documents
riskSignals: Customer seems confused about what NexaLink is or does
Request is clearly out of scope but customer is insistent
commonCustomerPhrases: Can you help me with [unrelated topic]?
Does NexaLink do [service not offered]?
I have a question that's a bit different

level0.name: Self-Service Resolution
level0.description: Customer resolves via app, portal, or IVR without agent involvement

level1.name: Frontline Agent
level1.description: Standard handling by Tier 1 agent or AI Agent
level1.authority: Credits up to $25, standard plan changes, basic troubleshooting, account info after verification

level2Supervisor.name: Tier 2 Supervisor
level2Supervisor.authority: Credits up to $100, retention offers from full catalog, exception approvals

level2TechnicalSpecialist.name: Technical Specialist
level2TechnicalSpecialist.authority: Network ticket creation, NOC coordination, advanced device diagnostics

level2BillingSpecialist.name: Billing Specialist
level2BillingSpecialist.authority: Credits up to $250, payment plan setup, refund approvals

level3Retention.name: Retention Specialist
level3Retention.authority: Credits up to $200, plan lock-in offers, premium add-on bundles

level3Compliance.name: Compliance and Regulatory Team
level3Compliance.authority: Handle regulatory complaints, PII breach response, legal demand letters

level3Fraud.name: Fraud Investigation Team
level3Fraud.authority: Account freeze, SIM block, fraud investigation, law enforcement liaison

level3NOC.name: Network Operations Center
level3NOC.description: Real-time network monitoring and incident management
level3NOC.authority: Declare outages, issue ETR, dispatch field teams

level4Business.name: Business Account Team
level4Business.authority: Contract modifications, SLA enforcement, custom plan negotiations

level4Legal.name: Legal Department
level4Legal.description: Active litigation, regulatory proceedings, demand letters

level4Executive.name: Executive Relations
level4Executive.description: Executive office customer relations for highest-priority matters

mandatory.condition: Customer explicitly requests supervisor or manager
mandatory.timeLimit: Must transfer within 3 minutes of request
mandatory.notes: Agent may not delay transfer to attempt further resolution after explicit supervisor request

mandatory.condition: Any legal threat — lawsuit, attorney mention, FCC/CPUC/CFPB complaint filing
mandatory.immediateAction: Document and transfer to Compliance team
mandatory.timeLimit: Document within 5 minutes; Compliance team must acknowledge within 4 business hours
mandatory.notes: Agent must not attempt to resolve legal threats at frontline level

mandatory.condition: Suspected fraud, unauthorized SIM swap, or account takeover
mandatory.immediateAction: Place account security hold and transfer to Fraud team
mandatory.notes: If verification fails due to possible account takeover, still create fraud case

mandatory.condition: Customer reports inability to reach 911 (emergency services access failure)
mandatory.immediateAction: Create P1 NOC ticket, advise alternate emergency access, supervisor notification
mandatory.notes: Customer safety is paramount — this overrides all other handling

mandatory.condition: Customer indicates suicidal ideation or intent to harm self or others
mandatory.immediateAction: Crisis protocol — provide 988 Lifeline, stay on call, supervisor notification
mandatory.notes: Do not abandon call; supervisor must be alerted immediately

mandatory.condition: Same issue reported 3 or more times without resolution
mandatory.immediateAction: Transfer to Tier 2 Supervisor with full case history
mandatory.notes: Third or subsequent repeat complaint is mandatory supervisor escalation
Frontline may only apply up to $25 credit; larger amounts require specialist

mandatory.condition: Identity verification fails twice and customer claims account ownership
mandatory.immediateAction: Lock account access; offer self-service PIN reset; log for potential fraud review
mandatory.notes: Do not grant account access after two verification failures regardless of circumstances

mandatory.condition: VIP customer with unresolved issue after standard handling
mandatory.immediateAction: Transfer to Tier 2 Supervisor; note as VIP priority
mandatory.notes: VIP customers should not wait for callback — live escalation preferred

mandatory.condition: Physical threat made by customer toward any NexaLink employee
mandatory.immediateAction: Terminate call immediately; log threat; notify supervisor and security
mandatory.notes: No warning needed before call termination when physical threat is made

conditional.condition: Churn risk score >= 70 AND frontline retention offers declined
conditional.notes: Do not transfer to retention if churn score is <70 and customer has not expressed cancellation intent

conditional.condition: Outage duration confirmed > 4 hours AND customer requests compensation
conditional.recommendedAction: Apply frontline credit up to $25; escalate to Billing Specialist for amounts above this
conditional.notes: Must confirm outage from NOC records before applying credit

conditional.condition: Business customer with SLA-relevant service issue
conditional.notes: Frontline should not calculate or promise SLA credits

conditional.condition: Abusive customer who has been warned twice and continues abuse
conditional.recommendedAction: Terminate call; valid underlying issue must still be documented
conditional.notes: Agent may terminate after two warnings; supervisor notification recommended but not required for call termination

conditional.condition: Customer disputes a previous agent's undocumented commitment
conditional.recommendedAction: Escalate to Supervisor for review; do not confirm or deny undocumented commitments
conditional.notes: Undocumented commitments cannot be honored without supervisor review

conditional.condition: Account ownership dispute between two parties
conditional.recommendedAction: Freeze account changes; route to Compliance team
conditional.notes: Neither party should receive account access until ownership is resolved

conditional.condition: International bill spike >$100 and customer denies travel
conditional.recommendedAction: Submit fraud investigation ticket; block additional international charges pending review
conditional.notes: Possible SIM-based fraud or unauthorized roaming

conditional.condition: Port-out request immediately following SIM swap within 48 hours
conditional.recommendedAction: Flag for Fraud review before processing port-out
conditional.notes: Combined SIM swap + port-out is a common fraud pattern

notEscalationButReviewRequired.action: Billing Specialist review; not urgent supervisor escalation
notEscalationButReviewRequired.notes: Submit specialist review ticket — does not require live supervisor transfer unless customer demands it

notEscalationButReviewRequired.condition: Customer uses aggressive tone but no actual threat
notEscalationButReviewRequired.notes: Not an escalation trigger in itself; apply de-escalation protocol

notEscalationButReviewRequired.condition: Social media threat made alongside valid issue
notEscalationButReviewRequired.action: Address underlying issue; log social media threat; do not escalate based on threat alone
notEscalationButReviewRequired.notes: Escalation should be based on issue merit, not social media threat

notEscalationButReviewRequired.condition: Customer makes vague reference to 'getting a lawyer' without explicit threat
notEscalationButReviewRequired.action: Continue handling underlying issue; document the remark; no mandatory Compliance escalation yet
notEscalationButReviewRequired.notes: Only mandatory escalation when legal action is explicitly stated or a demand letter is referenced

examples: Active account takeover with fraud in progress
P1_CRITICAL.escalationPath: Direct to supervisor and specialist simultaneously

P2_HIGH.responseTime: Within same call or within 2 business hours
P2_HIGH.escalationPath: Tier 2 Supervisor or appropriate specialist during current call

examples: Churn risk high with declined retention offer
P3_MEDIUM.responseTime: Within current call or within 1 business day
P3_MEDIUM.escalationPath: Specialist review ticket; offer callback or transfer

sentimentInteractionRules.FRUSTRATED: Increase empathy tone; acknowledge issue explicitly; avoid transactional language; offer explanation before solution
sentimentInteractionRules.ANGRY: Do not match tone; use de-escalation language; acknowledge feelings; focus on what CAN be done; if continues — consider supervisor transfer
sentimentInteractionRules.DISTRESSED: Slow down; simplify language; check for safety concerns; provide step-by-step guidance
sentimentInteractionRules.NEUTRAL: Professional and efficient; clear explanations; confirm understanding
sentimentInteractionRules.POSITIVE: Match energy appropriately; efficient handling; reinforce positive outcome
sentimentInteractionRules.ABUSIVE: Two-warning protocol; terminate if physical threat; do not reward with concessions

churnInteractionRules.CHURN_RISK_HIGH: Route to retention queue if available; use retention catalog; involve Retention Specialist if frontline offers declined
churnInteractionRules.CHURN_RISK_MEDIUM: Attempt retention with frontline offers; note in CRM; do not force retention pitch on customer who has not expressed intent to leave
churnInteractionRules.CHURN_RISK_LOW: Standard handling; proactive satisfaction check at end of call

vipHandlingModifiers.frontlineEscalationThreshold: Lower — VIP issues that are not immediately resolved should be escalated to supervisor sooner
vipHandlingModifiers.liveEscalationPreference: Live transfer preferred over callback for VIP customers

escalationDecisionExamples.scenario: Customer says 'I've been without service for 2 days and I'm going to sue NexaLink'
escalationDecisionExamples.decision: Mandatory Compliance escalation (legal threat) + Mandatory Technical escalation (2-day outage)
escalationDecisionExamples.notes: Handle both tracks simultaneously — Compliance for legal language, Technical for the service issue

escalationDecisionExamples.scenario: Customer fails verification twice and says 'Someone changed my PIN — I think I was hacked'
escalationDecisionExamples.decision: Mandatory Fraud escalation; account security hold; do not grant access
escalationDecisionExamples.notes: Account takeover scenario — failed verification + fraud claim = Fraud team

escalationDecisionExamples.scenario: Customer says 'I'm going to post this on TikTok' after being told their credit request was denied
escalationDecisionExamples.decision: NOT an escalation trigger in itself — continue handling underlying issue
escalationDecisionExamples.notes: Social media threat does not change resolution path; address underlying credit issue on its merits

escalationDecisionExamples.scenario: Customer has called 3 times about a slow data speed issue with no resolution
escalationDecisionExamples.decision: Mandatory supervisor escalation on third contact
escalationDecisionExamples.notes: Three or more contacts on same issue = mandatory supervisor; tag as REPEAT_ISSUE

escalationDecisionExamples.scenario: VIP customer is unhappy with their current plan and says 'I'm thinking of switching'
escalationDecisionExamples.decision: Immediate retention attempt with VIP retention catalog; escalate to Retention Specialist if declined
escalationDecisionExamples.notes: VIP churn risk is treated as P2 regardless of underlying issue severity

escalationReasonTemplates.legal: Customer stated: '[exact language]'. Logging as legal threat per policy. Routing to Compliance team.
escalationReasonTemplates.fraud: Suspected account fraud. Customer reported: [description]. Account security hold placed. Fraud case [number] created.
escalationReasonTemplates.repeatComplaint: This is the [X]rd contact for the same issue [description]. Mandatory supervisor escalation per repeat complaint policy.
escalationReasonTemplates.vipUnresolved: VIP customer with unresolved [issue type]. Standard handling exhausted. Transferring to Tier 2 Supervisor.
escalationReasonTemplates.beyondAuthority: Customer's request requires [authority level]. Transferring to [team name] with case notes.
escalationReasonTemplates.churnRiskHigh: Churn score [X]. Frontline retention offers declined. Transferring to Retention Specialist.
escalationReasonTemplates.crisisProtocol: Customer indicated [self-harm/harm to others]. Crisis protocol initiated. Supervisor notified.

escalationResponseTemplates.toCustomerBeforeTransfer: I want to make sure this gets the attention it deserves. I'm going to connect you with [a senior team member / our billing specialist / our compliance team] who is best positioned to help you with this. I'll brief them on your situation so you won't need to repeat yourself. One moment please.
escalationResponseTemplates.toReceivingAgent: Transferring [customer name] on account [number]. Issue: [description]. Actions taken: [summary]. Reason for escalation: [reason]. Customer sentiment: [FRUSTRATED/ANGRY/etc.]. Risk flags: [list]. Thank you.
escalationResponseTemplates.whenCustomerRefusesTransfer: I completely understand — I'll make sure I note this thoroughly so that our next steps are clearly visible to the team. What would you like to happen next? I want to find the best path for you.

privacyAndPIIHandling.piiDefinition: Any data that can identify a specific individual: full name combined with phone number, SSN, date of birth, account number, full address, payment card number, biometric data
privacyAndPIIHandling.verificationBeforeAnyPII: ABSOLUTE RULE — No PII may be disclosed, confirmed, or discussed before successful identity verification
privacyAndPIIHandling.maskedDataOnlyInSystem: Agents see only masked PII: last 4 of SSN, last 4 of card, abbreviated date of birth. Full PII is never displayed.
privacyAndPIIHandling.noPIIReadAloudOnCall: Agents must never read full PII aloud on a recorded call. Only confirm masked versions.
privacyAndPIIHandling.piiInEmailOrChat: Account numbers may be confirmed in secure chat sessions. Full SSN, card numbers, or passwords must never be typed or shared in chat.
privacyAndPIIHandling.thirdPartyPIIRestriction: Account data may only be shared with verified account owner or pre-registered authorized delegate

dataSubjectRights.accessRequest: Customers may request their data; direct to privacy@nexalink.com; 30-day response per applicable law
dataSubjectRights.deletionRequest: Customers may request data deletion (CCPA, state law); direct to privacy@nexalink.com; legal retention obligations apply
dataSubjectRights.correctionRequest: Contact info, billing address correctable by agent. SSN, name, DOB changes require in-store ID verification.

privacyAndPIIHandling.incidentReporting: Any suspected PII breach or unauthorized disclosure must be reported to privacy@nexalink.com and supervisor within 1 hour

verificationBeforeDisclosure.absoluteRule: No account information — plan, balance, usage, personal details — may be disclosed before successful identity verification
verificationBeforeDisclosure.exceptions: None. Not for urgency, VIP status, agent familiarity, or caller frustration.
verificationBeforeDisclosure.partialVerificationActions: If partial verification only — may confirm whether an account exists for a number; nothing more
verificationBeforeDisclosure.verificationMethodsReferenced: See POL-001 for full verification requirements

redactionRules.callTranscriptRedaction: All SSN digits, full card numbers, and full account passwords are auto-redacted in call transcripts by the transcription system
redactionRules.crmNotesRedaction: Agents must not type full SSN, card numbers, or passwords in CRM notes. Use masked versions only.
redactionRules.screenRecordingRedaction: If screen recording is active, agent must not navigate to any screen showing full PII
redactionRules.agentMustNeverRepeatFullPAN: Payment card Account Number (PAN) must never be repeated aloud, typed, or written by an agent

paymentInfoHandling.cardNumberCapture: NexaLink uses tokenized payment processing — agents never see full card numbers
paymentInfoHandling.cardUpdates: Customer provides new card directly into secure IVR or self-service portal; agents guide but do not capture
paymentInfoHandling.refundToCard: Refunds to original payment method processed by billing system using stored token; agent does not handle card details
paymentInfoHandling.fraudulentCharges: If card fraud is claimed, agent logs and routes to Fraud team; does not attempt to reverse card charges independently

legalRiskStatements.noLegalAdmissions: Agents and AI systems must never make statements that could be interpreted as admissions of liability
avoidPhrases: NexaLink is legally obligated to give you X

approvedAlternatives.insteadOf_WeWereWrong: I can see that this situation was not ideal, and I want to make it right.
approvedAlternatives.insteadOf_LegallyObligated: Our policy allows for the following in this situation...
approvedAlternatives.insteadOf_EntitledToCompensation: Based on the circumstances, I can submit a review for a service credit.

claimsTheAIMustNeverMake: Your data will never be accessed by anyone
NexaLink has the best network in [region]
You will definitely receive a refund/credit
The issue will be resolved by [date/time]
