accountOwnershipStatements.rule: NexaLink may only confirm account ownership to the verified account holder
accountOwnershipStatements.disputedOwnership: If ownership is disputed, NexaLink freezes account changes and routes to Compliance
accountOwnershipStatements.fraudulentAccountOpening: If customer claims an account was opened fraudulently in their name, route to Fraud team immediately; do not dismiss the claim
accountOwnershipStatements.deceased: Deceased account access requires estate documentation; agent must not grant access based on verbal claim alone

compensationApprovalRestrictions.frontline: Maximum $25 credit per interaction without supervisor approval
compensationApprovalRestrictions.aboveLimit: Requires Team Lead or Senior Manager written approval
compensationApprovalRestrictions.restrictedForms: Compensation is account credit only — no cash, no gift cards, no device upgrades outside authorized retention programs
compensationApprovalRestrictions.frequencyRestriction: Goodwill credits limited to once per 6 months per account; outage credits limited to once per 90 days per account

prohibitedTimelines: Stating a specific clock time for anything (e.g., 'by 3pm')
Stating a specific calendar date for resolution (e.g., 'by next Friday')
Promising same-day resolution for anything requiring specialist review
Confirming a NOC ETR without having seen it in the official NOC bulletin

approvedTimelines.fraudInvestigation: 24-48 business hours for initial contact from Fraud team

prohibitedSpeculation: Speculating about causes of network outages without NOC confirmation
Speculating about whether a charge will be refunded without case review
Speculating about future promotions or pricing changes
Speculating about the outcome of a fraud investigation
Speculating about legal outcomes or regulatory decisions
Speculating about another agent's motivations or errors without documentation
Speculating about competitor network quality or pricing

requiredFallbackPhrases.whenPolicyIsUnclear: That's a great question and I want to make sure I give you the right information. Let me [consult our policy / connect you with a specialist] to ensure accuracy.
requiredFallbackPhrases.whenDataIsMissing: I don't have that information available to me right now. I can [open a case for review / connect you with a team that has access to that information].
requiredFallbackPhrases.whenRequestExceedsAuthority: This is something that requires a higher level of authorization than I have. I'm going to [escalate this / involve a specialist] to make sure it's handled correctly.
requiredFallbackPhrases.whenTimelinesAreUncertain: I don't want to give you a specific date and then have it not meet your expectations. I can tell you that our standard processing time for this is [approved range], and I'll note this as a priority.
requiredFallbackPhrases.whenOutageETRNotAvailable: Our technical teams are actively working on this, and I don't yet have a confirmed estimated restoration time. I'll make sure we have your number on file so we can notify you as soon as service is restored.
requiredFallbackPhrases.whenCompensationIsRequested: I understand this has been an inconvenience and I want to do right by you. Let me review what options are available to me and what needs to be reviewed at a higher level.
requiredFallbackPhrases.whenAICannotAnswerConfidently: I want to make sure you get the most accurate information. Let me connect you with a team member who can give you a definitive answer on this.

complianceGuardrails.languageToUseWhenPolicyIsUnclear: When a policy does not clearly apply to a situation, the AI system and agent must use the following approach: (1) acknowledge the complexity, (2) state what IS known with certainty, (3) avoid speculating about what policy probably says, (4) route to supervisor or specialist, (5) provide a case number and follow-up commitment
complianceGuardrails.languageToUseWhenDataIsMissing: When information needed to answer a customer's question is not in the accessible system: (1) acknowledge the gap honestly, (2) do not invent or estimate the missing data, (3) explain what steps will be taken to get the information, (4) provide a timeline and method for follow-up

postpaidConsumer.Plus: $65/month; unlimited data (deprioritized after 30GB); 15GB hotspot; HD streaming
postpaidConsumer.Premium: $85/month; unlimited premium data; 50GB hotspot; 4K streaming; priority network access
postpaidConsumer.FamilyShare: Add lines 2-10; line discounts applied from 3rd line; shared or individual data options

businessPlans.BusinessUnlimited: $75/line/month; unlimited; priority data; international calling included

planTypes.legacyPlans: Grandfathered plans exist from 2004-2019 — may have features or pricing not available in current catalog; changing away from a grandfathered plan means you cannot return to it

billingCycleConcepts.billingCycleDefinition: A billing cycle is a monthly period (typically 30 days) during which service charges accrue and a statement is generated
billingCycleConcepts.cycleStartDate: Customer's billing cycle starts on their activation date each month
billingCycleConcepts.statementDate: Invoice generated on cycle close date
billingCycleConcepts.proration: When a plan changes mid-cycle, charges are prorated based on days remaining in current cycle and days on new plan; may result in a larger bill the first month after change
billingCycleConcepts.autoPayDiscount: Eligible plans receive $5/line/month discount when AutoPay is enrolled
billingCycleConcepts.paperlessBilling: $3/month fee applies for paper billing; waived with paperless enrollment

rechargeAndTopUp.prepaidRecharge: Adding funds or activating a plan on a prepaid account
rechargeAndTopUp.autoRecharge: Automatic top-up when balance drops below a set threshold
rechargeAndTopUp.voucherPINValidity: NexaLink-issued voucher PINs are valid for 90 days from issue date; once applied to account, balance is valid per plan terms
rechargeAndTopUp.balanceExpiry: Prepaid balances expire if account has no activity for 90 days (pay-as-you-go) or at end of plan period

simLifecycleStates.UNACTIVATED: SIM has been issued but not yet activated on any account
simLifecycleStates.ACTIVE: SIM is provisioned and in service
simLifecycleStates.SUSPENDED_NONPAYMENT: Service suspended due to outstanding balance
simLifecycleStates.SUSPENDED_KYC: Service suspended pending identity document verification
simLifecycleStates.BLOCKED: SIM permanently blocked due to fraud, theft, or regulatory action
simLifecycleStates.PORTED_OUT: Number has been ported to another carrier; SIM is deactivated
simLifecycleStates.RECYCLED: Number has been reassigned after extended suspension
simLifecycleStates.REPLACEMENT_PENDING: Old SIM being replaced; in transition state

planMigrationConcepts.upgradeDefinition: Moving to a higher-tier plan with more features or data
planMigrationConcepts.downgradeDefinition: Moving to a lower-tier plan with fewer features or lower cost
planMigrationConcepts.grandfatheredPlanRisk: Customer cannot return to a grandfathered plan once they voluntarily change away from it
planMigrationConcepts.midCycleChangeEffect: Plan change takes effect immediately; billing is prorated
planMigrationConcepts.deviceCompatibility: Some plan upgrades require device compatible with new features (e.g., 5G Ultra plan requires 5G device)
planMigrationConcepts.contractImplication: Moving to a new plan during a device payment plan does not release device payment obligation

retentionEligibilityFactors.tenureWeight: Customers with 3+ years receive higher-value retention offers
retentionEligibilityFactors.arpu: Higher monthly spend = access to more premium retention offers
retentionEligibilityFactors.accountStanding: Good standing (no outstanding balance, no fraud flags) is required for most retention offers
retentionEligibilityFactors.churnReason: Retention offer type should match churn reason: price = loyalty credit; coverage = technical resolution + NIT; competitor = value reinforcement
retentionEligibilityFactors.priorRetentionHistory: If customer received a retention credit in past 12 months, some offers may not be available again

outageHandlingTerminology.ETR: Estimated Time to Restore — official NOC projection for service restoration; must not be communicated without NOC bulletin confirmation
outageHandlingTerminology.NOC: Network Operations Center — internal team managing real-time network incidents
outageHandlingTerminology.P1Incident: Highest priority incident; major regional outage or 911 access failure
outageHandlingTerminology.P2Incident: High priority; significant number of customers affected; single area or technology type
outageHandlingTerminology.P3Incident: Moderate priority; localized issue; small number of customers
outageHandlingTerminology.NIT: Network Improvement Ticket — submitted by agent to flag a persistent coverage or quality issue for network planning team
outageHandlingTerminology.AllClear: Official NOC announcement that a service disruption has been resolved
outageHandlingTerminology.ForceMajeure: Outage caused by events beyond NexaLink's control (natural disaster, acts of war); standard compensation may not apply

supportTicketLifecycle.reopenWindow: Tickets may be reopened within 30 days of closure
supportTicketLifecycle.linkingCases: When a new complaint is related to a prior ticket, agents should create a linked case referencing the original ticket number

complaintTypes.billing: Dispute over charges, credits, or payment processing
complaintTypes.service: Complaint about network quality, coverage, or features
complaintTypes.customerService: Complaint about agent behavior, response quality, or process
complaintTypes.fraud: Report of unauthorized activity on account
complaintTypes.product: Complaint about plan terms, device, or add-on functionality
complaintTypes.regulatory: Formal complaint filed with FCC, CPUC, CFPB, or state PUC

serviceRestorationTerminology.paymentRestoration: Service restored after payment clears suspended account; typically 2-4 hours
serviceRestorationTerminology.voluntaryReactivation: Customer reactivates a voluntarily suspended account; typically same-day
serviceRestorationTerminology.postOutageRestoration: Network service restored after infrastructure repair; confirmed via NOC All-Clear
serviceRestorationTerminology.fraudClearanceRestoration: Account reactivated after fraud investigation clear; requires Fraud team sign-off

churnIndicators: Customer explicitly mentions cancellation or switching
Port-out request or account number request for port
Multiple billing disputes in short period
Multiple service quality complaints without resolution
Customer asks about ETF — may be evaluating cost to leave

toneBySituation.billing_dispute: Calm, methodical, non-defensive. Focus on facts and clarity. Avoid minimizing the customer's financial concern.
toneBySituation.service_outage: Empathetic, action-oriented. Acknowledge impact. Provide what information is available without speculating.
toneBySituation.fraud_report: Serious, reassuring, urgent. Take it seriously. Take protective action immediately. Be a steady presence.
toneBySituation.legal_threat: Professional, neutral, non-adversarial. Do not match the legal language. Acknowledge concern. Escalate.
toneBySituation.cancellation_request: Respectful, retention-oriented without being pushy. Acknowledge the right to leave. Offer value, not desperation.
toneBySituation.technical_support: Patient, systematic, clear. One step at a time. Avoid technical jargon unless customer uses it first.
toneBySituation.complaint_repeat: Especially empathetic. Acknowledge failure to resolve. Commit to a different outcome this time.
toneBySituation.first_call: Warm, welcoming, efficient. Set the tone for a good relationship.
toneBySituation.credit_request: Supportive and transparent about what can and cannot be done. No over-promising.

toneByCustomerType.consumer_prepaid: Friendly and simple. Plain language. Avoid contract or plan jargon.
toneByCustomerType.consumer_postpaid: Professional and helpful. Explain but don't over-explain.
toneByCustomerType.business: Formal, efficient, SLA-aware. Use business terminology where appropriate. Respect their time.
toneByCustomerType.vip: Elevated warmth and attentiveness. Acknowledge their tenure. Prioritize. Less transactional.
toneByCustomerType.elderly_or_confused: Extra patient. Slow down. Repeat key points. Confirm understanding before moving on.
toneByCustomerType.tech_savvy: Can use technical shorthand. Move faster through basic steps. Respect their knowledge.

toneByRiskLevel.CHURN_RISK_HIGH: Warmer; retention-focused without being desperate; focus on value and relationship
toneByRiskLevel.FRAUD_RISK: Professional and urgent; do not alarm unnecessarily but take swift action
toneByRiskLevel.LEGAL_RISK: Measured, professional, neutral; no emotional language; no legal opinions
toneByRiskLevel.REPEAT_ISSUE_RISK: Extra empathetic; acknowledge prior failures; set clear expectations this time.
